title: Senior Manager, Content & Product Marketing
description:
Summary of the Role

As Senior Manager of Content & Product Marketing at Maze, you'll be the architect of our content engine and product marketing strategy, creating compelling narratives that drive pipeline generation and revenue growth for our AI-powered cybersecurity platform. This is a unique opportunity to join as one of the early GTM team members of a well-funded startup building at the intersection of generative AI and cybersecurity, where you'll build our content function from the ground up while establishing product marketing practices that scale with hypergrowth.

You'll take full ownership of content strategy and execution-from working with security experts to create technical content in non-traditional formats, to crafting product positioning and sales enablement materials that accelerate deal velocity. Your success will be measured by revenue impact and pipeline generation, not content volume or vanity metrics. This role is perfect for someone who thinks creatively about content beyond traditional B2B playbooks, has strong security domain expertise or can quickly get up to speed, and wants the opportunity to own both content and product marketing as we scale from early traction to market leadership.

Within a year, you'll grow into a Director role leading a team as we expand our content and product marketing capabilities to support our next phase of growth.

Your Contributions to Our Journey

  • Create and execute breakthrough content strategies that move beyond standard blogs and ebooks-think podcasts, video series, technical exploit-style content, and other creative formats that establish Maze as a thought leader while generating qualified pipeline through storytelling that resonates with technical security buyers
  • Execute directly on content creation by working with security experts and internal engineering teams to translate complex AI and security concepts into compelling narratives, while simultaneously developing the strategy and processes that will scale our content function as we grow
  • Define and evolve our product messaging, positioning, and go-to-market strategy for our agentic vulnerability management platform, ensuring every product launch and feature release drives measurable customer adoption and revenue impact
  • Develop comprehensive sales enablement materials including battle cards, competitive positioning, demo scripts, product training, and customer success stories that accelerate deal velocity and improve win rates across our sales organization
  • Support and contribute to high-impact projects including our website relaunch and Q1 remediation product launch, bringing product marketing discipline to ensure successful execution from concept through launch
  • Establish and maintain high standards across all marketing outputs-from planning product launches to ensuring every comma is in the right place-setting the bar for excellence as we scale
  • Work in close partnership with sales leadership, product teams, and engineering to ensure marketing efforts support revenue goals, with daily collaboration across US and European time zones to maintain alignment and momentum

What You Need to Be Successful

  • 5-8 years in B2B SaaS marketing with proven track record creating non-traditional content that breaks from standard B2B playbooks-we want to see examples of novel formats you've pioneered (podcasts, video series, unconventional campaigns) that drove meaningful business impact, not just "I wrote blogs and ebooks"
  • Strong understanding of cloud security, vulnerability management, CVEs, and security workflows, OR demonstrated ability to quickly master complex technical domains and translate them into compelling content. Ideally you can discuss technical security concepts fluently with engineering teams and customers-this is a top priority for success in the role.
  • Hands-on experience in both content creation and product marketing execution-you can come from either background but must have proven success in both functions, with examples of building content strategies AND launching products that drove pipeline and revenue
  • Track record at high-growth companies where you've built innovative campaigns with progressive marketing leadership, demonstrating ability to think differently about marketing and execute creative approaches that others might not approve
  • Meticulous standards for quality across all work-from strategic planning of product launches to ensuring perfect execution of every deliverable-you understand that details matter and consistently hold yourself to the highest bar
  • Comfort creating content strategies and product marketing programs from scratch while also being hands-on with execution-you're 100% player-coach at this stage, not someone who just manages freelancers or delegates execution
  • Ability to instill confidence in experienced sales teams and collaborate with strong-willed executives while remaining a genuine team player-you're knowledgeable and decisive without being an asshole
  • Experience tying marketing activities directly to pipeline generation and revenue outcomes, with ability to set and measure success metrics that matter to the business, not just activity-based KPIs
  • Nice to Haves: Experience at vulnerability management, risk platforms, or technical detection tool companies (EDR, NDR, SIEM); background in backend or infrastructure engineering with transition to product marketing; track record creating video content, podcasts, or multimedia campaigns that generated measurable business impact; experience with AI/ML product marketing or agent-based systems; previous startup experience scaling marketing from Series A through hypergrowth

Why Join Us

  • Ambitious Challenge: We're using generative AI (LLMs and agents) to solve some of the most pressing challenges in cybersecurity today. You'll be building the marketing engine at the cutting edge of this field, creating content and positioning that defines a new category of AI-powered security solutions.
  • Expert Team: We are a team of hands-on leaders with experience in Big Tech and Scale-ups. Our team has been part of the leadership teams behind multiple acquisitions and an IPO.
  • Impactful Work: Cybersecurity is a force for good - helping to stop cyber attacks ultimately helps deliver better outcomes for all of us. Your content and positioning will directly help security teams discover and adopt solutions that protect organizations worldwide.
  • Build an AI-Native Company: We're building a new company in the AI era with the opportunity to design everything from the ground up - you'll pioneer AI-assisted marketing practices while building content and product marketing functions that scale with hypergrowth.

Privacy policyDate of publication: 07-06-2024We at Koto manage our employer branding and recruitment process through our career site (the "Career Site"), and by using a related applicant tracking system.In this privacy policy, we explain how we process your personal data if: You visit our Career Site (you being a "Visitor") You connect with us via our Career Site, to create a profile with us and receive information about current or future vacancies with us (you being a "Connecting Candidate") You apply for a position with us, via our Career Site or a third party service (you being an "Applying Candidate") We collect information about you from other parties, sites and services, since we believe your profile is of interest for our current or future vacancies (you being a "Sourced Candidate") We receive information about you from our employees or partners, since they believe your profile is of interest for our current or future vacancies (you being a "Referred Candidate") We receive information about you from a Candidate, who lists you as their reference (you being a "Reference"). This privacy policy also describes what rights you have when we process your personal data, and how you can exercise these rights.When we use the term "Candidate" in this privacy policy, we are referring to each of Connecting Candidates; Applying Candidates; Sourced Candidates; and Referred Candidates, unless it's stated otherwise.1. About processing of personal dataPersonal data is all information that can be directly or indirectly linked to a living, physical person. Examples of personal data are: name, e-mail address, telephone number and IP address. Processing of personal data is any automated use of personal data - such as collecting, creating, analyzing, sharing, and deleting personal data.There are laws and regulations on how companies may process personal data, so-called data protection laws. Different data protection laws apply to different types of use of personal data, and in different parts of the world. An example of a data protection law that is relevant for our use of your personal data, as described in this privacy policy, is the EU Data Protection Regulation (2016/679, "GDPR"). Most obligations under the GDPR apply to the so-called data controller. A data controller is the entity that decides for which purposes personal data will be processed, and how the processing will be executed. The data controller can use a so-called data processor. A data processor is an entity that is only allowed to process personal data as instructed by the data controller, and may not use the personal data for its own purposes.We are the data controller when we process your personal data as described in this Privacy policy.2. What personal data do we process?All individuals Device information - If you visit our Career Site, we will collect information about your device, such as IP address, browser type and version, session behaviour, traffic source, screen resolution, preferred language, geographic location, operating system and device settings/usage. Technical and statistical data - If you visit our Career Site, we will collect technical and statistical data about your use of the site, such as information about which URLs you visit, and your activity on the site. Communications data - We will collect and store your communication with us, including the information you provided in the communication. This may include the content of emails, video recordings, messages on social media, the information you add to your account with us, surveys, etc. Contact details - Such as your name, email address, telephone number and physical address. Candidates Data from interviews, assessments and other information from the recruitment process - Such as notes from interviews with you, assessments and tests made, salary requirements. Information in your application - Such as your CV, cover letter, work samples, references, letters of recommendation and education. Information in your public profile - Meaning the information we collect about you from public sources related to your professional experience, such as LinkedIn or the website of your current employer. Information provided by references - Meaning the information we receive from our employees or partners who refer you to us, or by the persons you have listed as your references. 3. Where do we receive your personal data from?All individuals From the Career Site. If you visit our Career Site, we collect technical and statistical information about how you use the Career Site, and information from your device. Directly from you. Most of the information we process about you, we receive directly from you, for example when you apply for a position with us or connect with us. You can always choose not to provide us with certain information. However, some personal data is necessary in order for us to process your application or provide you the information you request to get from us. References From the person for whom you are a reference. If a Candidate lists you as their reference, we will collect your contact details from the candidate to be able to contact you. Candidates From public sources. We may collect personal data about you from public sources, such as LinkedIn or the website of your current employer. From our references. We may receive information about you from our employees or partners (such as recruitment service providers), when they believe your profile is of interest for our current or future vacancies. From your references. If you provide us with references, we may collect information about you from them. Data we create ourselves or in cooperation with you. Information about your application and profile is usually created by us, or by us in cooperation with you, during the recruitment process. This may for example include notes from interviews with you, assessments and tests made. 4. For what purposes do we process your personal data?Protect and enforce our rights, interests and the interests of others, for example in connection with legal claims.Affected individuals: The individual(s) affected by the legal issue - this may include persons from all categories of individuals listed above.Categories of personal data used: All the categories of personal data listed above can be used for this purpose.Share your personal data with other recipients, for the purposes mentioned in Section 5 below.Affected individuals: Varies depending on the purpose of the sharing, see Section 5 below.Categories of personal data used: All the categories of personal data listed above may be used for this purpose.Collect information about your use of the Career Site, using cookies and other tracking technologies, as described in our Cookie Policy.Affected individuals: Visitors.Categories of personal data used: Device information.Maintain, develop, test, and otherwise ensure the security of the Career Site.Affected individuals: Visitors.Categories of personal data used: Device information; Technical and statistical data.Analyse how the Career Site and its content is being used and is performing, to get statistics and to improve operational performance.Affected individuals: Visitors.Categories of personal data used: Device information; Technical and statistical data.Provide you with updates about vacancies with us.Affected individuals: Connecting Candidates. Categories of personal data used: Contact details; Communications data.Review profiles and applications sent to us. This also includes communicating with you about your application and profile. Affected individuals: Connecting Candidates; Applying Candidates. Categories of personal data used: All the categories of personal data listed above may be used for this purpose.Collect and evaluate your professional profile on our own initiative. This also includes communicating with you regarding your profile.Affected individuals: Sourced Candidates; Referred Candidates.Categories of personal data used: All the categories of personal data listed above may be used for this purpose.Contact you directly about specific, future vacancies with us.Affected individuals: Candidates. Categories of personal data used: All the categories of personal data listed above may be used for this purpose.Record the interview(s) with you. Affected individuals: Candidates. Categories of personal data used: Communications data. Contact you to ask for your participation in surveysAffected individuals: Candidates. </p Categories of personal data used: All the categories of personal data listed above may be used for this purpose.Contact you to ask you to provide information about a Candidate, and evaluate the information you provide. Affected individuals: References.Categories of personal data used: Contact details; Communications data. 5. Whom do we share your personal data with?Our service providers. We share your personal data with our suppliers who provide services and functionality in our employer branding- and recruitment process. For example, this includes recruitment service providers and the supplier of our Career Site and related applicant tracking system. Our group companies. We share your personal data with our group companies, when they provide us services and functionality to our employer branding- and recruitment process, such as access to particular systems and software. Companies providing cookies on the Career Site. If you consent to it, cookies are set by other companies than us, who will use the data collected by these cookies in accordance with their own privacy policy. You can find information about which cookies this applies to in our Cookie Policy. To authorities and other public actors - when we are ordered to do so. We will share your personal data with authorities and other public actors when we have a legal obligation to do so. To parties involved in legal proceedings. If needed to protect or defend our rights, we share your personal data with public authorities or with other parties involved in a potential or existing legal proceeding. This can for example be in case of discrimination claims.Mergers and acquisitions etc. In connection with a potential merger, sale of company assets, financing, or acquisition of all or part of our business to another company, we may share your personal data to other parties involved in the process. 6. On what legal bases do we process your personal data?To be able to process your personal data, we need to have a so-called legal basis. A legal basis is a reason for processing the personal data that is justified under the GDPR.When we process your personal data for the purposes described in this Privacy Policy, the legal basis we rely on is normally that the processing is necessary for our legitimate interest in being able to recruit talent with the relevant competence for us. We have concluded that we have a legitimate interest in being able to perform the personal data processing for this purpose; that the processing is necessary to achieve that purpose; and that our interest outweighs your right not to have your data processed for this purpose. You can contact us for more information about how this assessment was made. See Section 9 and 10 below for our contact information.There may be specific circumstances when the processing is only performed if and when you provide your consent to the processing. This is for example the case if we propose to record an interview with you. Please see Section 9 below for more information about your right to withdraw your consent. 7. When do we transfer your personal data outside of the EU/EEA, and how do we protect it then?We always strive to process your personal data within the EU/EEA area. However, some of our service providers process your personal data outside of the EU/EEA. We also use suppliers whose parent company, or whose subcontractor's parent company, is based outside the EU/EEA. In these cases, we have taken into account the risk that the personal data may be disclosed to countries outside the EU/EEA, for example because of an authority request.In cases where another recipient of your personal data (as described in Section 5 above) is based outside the EU/EEA, this will also mean that your personal data is transferred outside the EU/EEA. When we, or one of our suppliers, transfer your personal data outside the EU/EEA, we will ensure that a safeguard recognized by the GDPR is used to enable the transfer. We use the following safeguards: A decision by the EU Commission that the country outside of the EU/EEA to which your personal data is transferred has an adequate level of protection, which corresponds to the level of protection afforded by the GDPR. In particular, we rely on the EU Commission's adequacy decision for the US via the so-called EU-US Data Privacy Framework, and the adequacy decision for the UK. Entering into the EU Commission's standard clauses with the recipient of the personal data outside the EU/EEA. This means that the recipient guarantees that the level of protection for your personal data afforded by the GDPR still applies, and that your rights are still protected. When your personal data is transferred outside the EU/EEA, we also implement appropriate technical and organizational safeguards, to protect the personal data in case of a disclosure. Exactly which protective measures we implement depends on what is technically feasible, and sufficiently effective, for the particular transfer.If you want more information about the cases in which your personal data is transferred outside the EU/EEA you can contact us using the contact details in Section 9 and 10 below.8. For how long do we keep your personal data?All individualsIf we process your personal data for the purpose of being able to protect and enforce our rights, we will keep your personal data until the relevant legal issue has been fully and finally resolved. VisitorsWe keep your personal data for one (1) year for security purposes. The retention periods for cookies are set out in our Cookie Policy. We keep your personal data to analyse the performance of the Career Site for as long as we keep personal data about you for other purposes. CandidatesIf you are a Connecting Candidate (only), we keep your personal data for as long as you remain connected with us.For other types of Candidates, we keep your personal data to decide if you are a suitable candidate for the relevant vacancy(ies) with us. If you don't succeed in the initial recruitment process, we keep your personal data for as long as needed to consider, and potentially contact you, for relevant future job openings.If you are hired, we will keep your personal data during your employment, for other purposes than those stated above, which you will be informed of. ReferencesWe keep your personal data for as long as we keep the personal data of the Candidate for whom you acted as a reference. 9. What rights do you have, and how can you exercise them?In this section, you will find information about the rights you have when we process your personal data. As described below, some of the rights only come into play when we process your personal data under a particular legal basis. If you want to exercise any of the rights listed here, we suggest that you: Visit the Data & Privacy page on our Career Site, where we offer features to let you exercise your rights; Log in to your account with us, where you can use the settings in the account to exercise your rights; or Contact us directly at bex@koto.studio. Right to be informedYou have the right to be informed about how we process your personal data. You also have the right to be informed if we plan to process your personal data for any purpose other than that for which it was originally collected. We provide you with such information through this privacy policy, through updates on our Career Site (see also Section 11 below), and by answering any questions you may have for us. Right to access your personal data. You have the right to know if we process personal data about you, and to receive a copy of the data we process about you. In connection with receiving the copy of your data, you will also receive information about how we process your personal data. Right to access and to request a transfer of your personal data to another recipient ("data portability").You can request a copy of the personal data relating to you that we process for the performance of a contract with you, or based on your consent, in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format. This will allow you to use this data somewhere else, for example to transfer it to another recipient. If technically feasible, you also have the right to request that we transfer your data directly to another recipient.Right to have your personal data deleted ("right to be forgotten").In some cases, you have the right to have us delete personal data about you. This is for example the case if it's no longer necessary for us to process the data for the purpose for which we collected it; if you withdraw your consent; if you have objected to the processing and there are no legitimate, overriding justifications for the processing. (For the separate right to object, see below.) Right to object against our processing of your personal data.You have the right to object to processing of your personal data which is based on our legitimate interest, by referencing your personal circumstances. Right to restrict processing. If you believe that the personal data we process about you is inaccurate, that our processing is unlawful, or that we don't need the information for a specific purpose, you have the right to request that we restrict the processing of such personal data. If you object to our processing, as described just above, you can also request us to restrict processing of that personal data while we make our assessment of your request. When our processing of your personal data is restricted, we will (with the exception of storage) only process the data with your consent or for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims, to protect the rights of another natural or legal person, or for reasons relating to an important public interest. Right to rectification.You have the right to request that we rectify inaccurate information, and that we complete information about you that you consider incomplete.Right to withdraw your consent.When we process your personal data based on your consent, you have the right to withdraw that consent at any time. If you do so, we will stop processing your data for the purposes you've withdrawn your consent for. However, it doesn't affect the lawfulness of processing that was based on your consent before it was withdrawn.Right to raise a complaint.If you have complaints about our processing of your personal data, you can raise a complaint with the data protection authority in United Kingdom. You can find their contact details here.You can also lodge a complaint with your national data protection authority, which you can find listed here if you are based in the EU. If you are based in the UK, you can lodge a complaint with the Information Commissioner's Office, here.10. Where can you turn with comments or questions?If you want to get in touch with us to exercise your rights, or if you have any questions, comments or concerns about how we handle your personal data, you can reach us by sending an email to bex@koto.studio. 11. Updates to this Privacy policyWe update this privacy policy when necessary - for example, because we start processing your personal data in a new way, because we want to make the information even clearer to you, or if it's necessary to do so in order to comply with applicable data protection laws.We encourage you to regularly check this page for any changes. You can always check the top of this page to see when this privacy policy was last updated.

title: Production Designer (Freelance)
description:
Overview

Looking for a freelance production designer to join our fantastic team in Los Angeles. You'll have the opportunity to work with a major entertainment client, collaborating closely with a talented group of individuals. Your main responsibilities will include adapting images and artwork to precise specifications, as well as creating in-situ mock-ups. If you possess an impeccable attention to detail, consistently deliver top-notch work, and have a knack for responding swiftly and efficiently, we would be thrilled to welcome you aboard. This role calls for a motivated self-starter who can work autonomously and effectively manage their time.

Responsibilities

  • Create very detailed and complex in-situ mock-up decks.
  • Take existing client art and adapt that to spec.
  • Demand excellence and have a clear POV on quality.
  • Be able to navigate complex templates and specifications.
  • Assist with file delivery to multiple platforms.
  • Several quick turnarounds cross-vertically.

Requirements

  • At least 2 years of experience working in digital production and graphic design in a creative environment, such as an agency or in-house, preferably for an entertainment, or consumer brand
  • Demonstrated proficiency in Adobe Photoshop
  • Proficiency with Google Slides.
  • Exceptionally organized and values established processes
  • Expertise in all aspects of graphic design with a strong ability to create high-quality creative

description:

title: Senior Copywriter - (m/f/d) MILAN
description:
About the Role

The Senior Copywriter leads the verbal identity of the work, owning tone of voice, storytelling and written craft across all channels. As a native English speaker, you bring precision, nuance and originality to every word - from big platform ideas to the smallest line of copy.

Key Responsibilities

About You

Applications close Monday, March 16, 2026.

title: Copywriter - (m/f/d) MILAN
description:

title: Producer / Project Manager (Design and Email)
description:
About the Role

Los Angeles based resources only. Only online submissions will be considered, please don't contact anyone directly.

title: Graphic Designer
description:

title: Growth Bid Writer
description:
About the Role

As a Growth Bid Writer, you will lead the development of compelling, compliant, and market‑specific proposals, ranging from expressions of interest to full commercial tender submissions, ensuring they effectively meet and exceed client requirements. This includes analysing tender documentation, translating complex delivery approaches into clear and persuasive copy, and collaborating with internal experts and market leads across global offices.

Responsibilities

  • Focus on bid procurement (writing) for UK government clients and/or EU or European country clients.
  • Writing and editorial oversight on commercial tender submissions, as well as developing proposals for expressions of interest.
  • Analysing and assessing commercial tender requirements and developing copy that successfully scores maximum points available on those requirements.
  • Developing new business proposals that are market specific and compliant as well as compelling.
  • Attending market engagement events to engage on potential tender requirements.
  • Liaising and engaging market-based business leads to develop persuasive reports and presentations for generating business opportunities.
  • Engaging with in-house experts to translate complex delivery ideas and mechanisms into highly readable and understandable content.

About You

The successful candidate will bring bid writing experience, producing clear, persuasive, and compliant proposals for public‑sector or international clients. They will be an exceptional written communicator with strong attention to detail, able to translate complex ideas into accessible content while managing multiple deadlines in a fast‑paced environment. Fluent in English with excellent proofreading skills, they will work effectively both independently and collaboratively, with additional European languages, EMEA bid‑winning experience, or knowledge of behaviour‑change communications considered strong advantages.

You'll bring

  • Proven professional experience of bid writing, with exposure to international environments and non-commercial clients.
  • Demonstrable skills in proposal writing, sub-editing and effective communicating through the written word.
  • Fluent in written and spoken English with excellent proof-reading skills.
  • Strong attention to detail.
  • Evidence of planning and seeing written work through to the end.
  • Able to work both independently and collaboratively in groups.
  • Ability to work in a high paced, results-oriented environment.

Bonus points if you have

  • Record of winning on written tender bids within the EMEA market.
  • An understanding of the role of communications in behaviour change.
  • Ability to triage and manage a number of concurrent bids being prepared.
  • Fluent in an additional European language.

What You Get

A competitive salary and benefits package that includes 27 days holiday per year, pension contributions, private healthcare, discounted dental insurance, enhanced maternity/parental leave, plus a host of other benefits including cultural stimulation allowance, half days off before bank holidays, emergency care days for dependants, volunteering leave, emergency leave, season ticket loan, payroll giving, social events, and more. We also support diversity and inclusion initiatives, learning opportunities, mentoring programmes, Employee Led Networks, and personal development to set you up for success.

About M+C Saatchi World Services

M+C Saatchi World Services is a division of M+C Saatchi Group, the world's largest independent communications network. Our specialists work across six continents, combining global expertise with local nuance to address complex issues in defence, development, diplomacy, homeland security, health, and more.

title: Visual Communications Designer
description:
About the Role

Miro needs a dedicated, senior-level Visual Communications Designer. The immediate priority is Canvas 26-our flagship annual event across four cities-where you'll own the keynote deck end-to-end. But this is a full-time role with a much wider remit: executive and CMO communications, board decks, all-hands, investor materials, and sales enablement. You'll work closely with senior leadership including the CMO, embedded directly in the Miro moments that count.

What you'll do

  • Own the Canvas 26 keynote end-to-end across four cities - San Francisco, London, Tokyo, and Sydney - adapting and iterating as speakers and content evolve
  • Design and deliver all executive and CMO presentations, board decks, all-hands, and sales enablement materials
  • Work directly with senior leadership to shape and refine presentations in real time
  • Transform complex product narratives, strategy, and data into clear, compelling visual stories
  • Build and maintain a presentation system - templates, assets, and standards - that brings consistency across the business
  • Push the craft - typography, layout, data visualisation, animation - and set a new bar for what presentation design looks like at Miro
  • Your work will be seen by thousands of people across four global cities - this is as high visibility as presentation design gets

What you'll need

  • 5+ years of presentation design experience working with C-suite or senior leadership stakeholders
  • A portfolio demonstrating range across keynotes, executive decks, data visualisation, and event content
  • Expert-level Keynote skills including animations, transitions, and build orders
  • Strong working knowledge of Figma, Illustrator, and Photoshop
  • The ability to work fast without losing craft quality, managing multiple priorities simultaneously
  • A strong creative POV and the confidence to defend it-and the good sense to know when to let it go
  • Comfortable with ambiguity, last-minute changes, and the pace that comes with working directly alongside senior leadership

What's in it for you

We want you to feel supported, connected, and ready to grow. Our global benefits package generally includes equity, a wellbeing benefit, a WFH equipment allowance, and an annual Learning & Development stipend. Join a diverse team where you can do your best work. Full benefits may differ per location. If you would like to learn more about location-specific benefits, please refer to our Global Miro benefits board.

Miro is a visual workspace for innovation that enables distributed teams of any size to build the next big thing. The platform's infinite canvas enables teams to lead engaging workshops and meetings, design products, brainstorm ideas, and more. Miro, co-headquartered in San Francisco and Amsterdam, serves more than 100M users and 250,000 companies collaborate in the Innovation Workspace. Miro was founded in 2011 and currently has more than 1,600 employees in 13 hubs around the world.

We are a team of dreamers. We look for individuals who dream big, work hard, and above all stay humble. Collaboration is at the heart of what we do and through our work together we hope to create a supportive, welcoming, and innovative environment. We strive to play as a team to win the world and create a better version of ourselves every day. If this sounds like something that excites you, we want to hear from you!

  • 02. Hiring Manager

    The hiring manager will meet to discuss your experience and fit for the role by assessing whether you align with the Miro Mindset (Curiosity, Drive, Resilience, Empathy, Cognitive Agility & Accountability). This interview usually takes 90-120 minutes.

    An interactive presentation to see your skills in action, based around a particular case study or business problem.

title: Graduate Product Designer
description:
About the Team

At Miro, the design team is fully embedded in various product streams while operating as a tight-knit, centralized team of designers, researchers & content writers. While our processes are fully consolidated into the product development life cycle, we still enjoy a high degree of ownership and autonomy in our work. We're striving to find the right balance between visual craftsmanship, user needs, and business goals.

You'll apply your experience and education with a customer-centric mindset to solve customer problems and identify business opportunities.

We operate in a fast-paced environment and get to have a direct impact on the millions of users who rely on Miro to collaborate and create the next big thing. As a part of our Product Design team, you will contribute to our design process, culture, and experience principles and set new standards for design quality. Learn more about the colorful design team and our culture: https://miro.design/

About the Role

The Miro Graduate Product Design Programme is designed to help new designers launch their careers in design, research or content writing. In our 12-month programme, you'll gain hands-on experience working on real-world projects. You'll also be mentored by experienced designers, researchers, and content writers, who will help you develop your skills and career.

As a graduate product designer at Miro, you will focus on learning, growing and bringing a new fresh perspective to the Miro experience. Your goal will be to explore the customer experience, identify opportunities, explore the fields of product design, UXR, and content design, and deliver high-quality solutions but most of all to learn and grow as a new designer at Miro and find your place from Product Design, Research, Content Design or maybe somewhere else.

What you'll do

  • Explore the end-to-end design process, from discovery research to effective problem framing, ideation, co-creation, prototyping, and user testing, into high-fidelity designs

  • Understand the success of your solutions with the team and iterate on them closely with engineering partners to ensure solid implementation in production.
  • Understand the competitive landscape, and using qualitative and quantitative methods hypothesize new solutions and improvements to the Miro products.
  • Bring delight to Miro users through customer-centric solutions implemented through high-quality interaction design and creative visuals.

What you'll need

  • Graduate in 2025-6 in Bachelor's or Master's in interaction design, industrial design, creative design, HCI, or a related design field
  • Strong drive to learn, and absorb knowledge from your team, and ability to take and utilize this knowledge in your work
  • AI-First Proficiency - We're building for an AI-first future, and we're looking for graduates who aren't just using AI-they're mastering it.
  • Effective written and verbal communication skills

title: Senior Content Designer
description:
About the Team

We have a small but mighty content design team at Miro, with just three people across multiple key initiatives. This means we must be flexible, resilient and good at prioritising projects where we can have the biggest impact! We're an integral part of the Design org, shaping the user experience for some of our most impactful initiatives. We operate within a hybrid model, embedding deeply within high-priority cross-functional teams to craft user-centered content that guides and delights our users, and offering light support to lower priority work.

About the Role

We're looking for a talented Content Designer and AI whizz to join our team and contribute directly to high-impact projects that elevate the Miro experience. This role will sit within our AI Canvas team, specifically working on enhancing our AI Workflow experiences. You'll partner with senior leadership across product, design, and engineering, and build the frameworks that help other teams create consistent AI content at scale.

You'll also be a key player in shaping the content design discipline at Miro, collaborating closely with product managers, designers, researchers, and product marketers and delivering content that enhances usability and drives adoption. This is an exciting opportunity to make a meaningful impact on our product and be part of growing the maturity of our content design practice.

What you'll do

  • Work with product, UX, and engineering teams to help shape the product vision and create user-focused content for high-priority AI features and initiatives - ensuring clarity and consistency across the product
  • Design and develop prompts, UI copy and error messages to help our users adopt Sidekicks and Flows and get high quality results when using them
  • Plan, develop and deliver conversational content that guides users effectively, reduces cognitive load, and aligns with Miro's voice and tone guidelines
  • Contribute to the refinement and application of Miro's content design guidelines, particularly focused on refining our AI principles and guidance which will ultimately need to live in our design system and MCPs
  • Collaborate with design and UX research to conduct user research, test content hypotheses, and iterate solutions based on data and feedback
  • Participate in design and product reviews, providing clear rationale for your content design decisions
  • Advocate for the value of content design, helping to integrate the discipline into the broader product development process

On a typical day you'll be:

  • Writing and testing system prompts, then debugging the model
  • Defining evaluation criteria with engineering and reviewing AI outputs against quality benchmarks
  • Shaping conversation flows and interaction patterns in design crits and tiger teams
  • Running through edge cases to figure out where the AI breaks and how to recover gracefully
  • Creating conversation starters and prompt suggestions that teach users what's actually possible

What you'll need

  • 6+ years of experience in content design or UX writing, with a strong background in creating content for digital products
  • A strong portfolio showcasing your ability to create clear, user-focused content and demonstrating the impact on user experience and business goals
  • Proven experience working closely with cross-functional teams, including product management, UX design, and engineering, to deliver seamless content solutions
  • Deep understanding of UX writing principles, information architecture, and user-centered design methodologies
  • Familiarity with AI products, conversation design, or prompt engineering (you don't need to be an engineer, but you should be comfortable getting hands-on with how AI systems behave)
  • Ability to align product content strategy with business objectives, balancing user needs with technical feasibility
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to articulate the value of content design and collaborate effectively across diverse teams
  • A proactive and flexible approach, comfortable working autonomously in a fast-paced, sometimes ambiguous environment and managing multiple priorities

What's in it for you

We want you to feel supported, connected, and ready to grow. Our global benefits package generally includes equity, a wellbeing benefit, a WFH equipment allowance, and an annual Learning & Development stipend. Join a diverse team where you can do your best work. Full benefits may differ per location. title: PRODUCT DESIGNER
description:
About the Role

Pininfarina, prestigioso gruppo multinazionale, market leader in servizi di Stile nel settore Automotive, Product & Experience e Architecture design, per la sua sede di Cambiano (TO), ricerca un/una: PRODUCT DESIGNER. La risorsa avrà il compito di assicurare il miglioramento continuo della qualità delle attività tecniche (studi di fattibilità e progettazione) e degli output relativi, svolgendo sia un'azione di coaching sulle risorse del team sia partecipando alle riunioni di avanzamento dei progetti, promuovendo e verificando lo svolgimento degli studi di fattibilità.

Responsibilities

  • Assicurare il supporto tecnico alla progettazione: Collaborare con il team nella realizzazione di progetti, garantendo la precisione nella progettazione sia in 2D che in 3D e contribuendo alla definizione di soluzioni formali e funzionali. Individuare soluzioni di produzione efficaci.
  • Supporto nella gestione del cliente: Partecipare agli incontri con i clienti, ascoltando attivamente e ponendo domande pertinenti per guidarli verso i piani di lavoro più efficaci ed economici.
  • Gestione autonoma di aree di responsabilità: Coordinare in autonomia piccole design challenge (1-5 gg), all'interno di progetti, o progetti di piccola scala, garantendo l'esecuzione nei tempi e secondo gli standard aziendali e coordinando i fornitori.
  • Elaborazione grafica: Produrre sketch e render di immagini work-in-progress per discussioni interne, supportando il team nella comunicazione visiva delle idee progettuali.

Requirements

  • Titolo di studio: Laurea in Industrial design o campi correlati.
  • Esperienza: Esperienza in progetti che coprono l'intero processo progettuale, dalla definizione delle esigenze degli stakeholder alla fase di prototipazione.
  • Progettazione tecnica: Competenza nella progettazione 2D e 3D, con una profonda comprensione degli strumenti e dei software necessari per il completamento delle attività.
  • Elaborazione di soluzioni progettuali: Capacità di concettualizzare direzioni progettuali formali e funzionali senza necessità di supervisione.
  • Scrittura e comunicazione: capacità di scrittura chiara, espressiva e concisa, senza necessità di revisione.
  • Lingue: Inglese fluente, con capacità di esprimersi in contesti professionali e presentare di fronte a piccoli gruppi.
  • Giudizio e autonomia: Capacità di identificare ostacoli e opportunità, proponendo soluzioni in autonomia.
  • Coordinamento: Collaborazione con il team per migliorare il lavoro complessivo del progetto e supporto ai colleghi.
  • Leadership: Capacità di difendere le scelte progettuali, e supporto a designer junior.
  • Ownership: Definizione autonoma della direzione del lavoro, richiesta spontanea di check-in in caso di necessità.
  • Comunicazione: Contributo chiave alla pianificazione dei progetti e rappresentazione verso l'esterno con supporto minimo.
  • Feedback: Ricezione di feedback orientata al miglioramento, fornitura di feedback costruttivi ai designer junior per migliorare la loro conoscenza e competenza.
  • Contributo: Espressione di un punto di vista progettuale forte, con flessibilità nell'incorporare le idee altrui e migliorare il lavoro complessivo.

Il presente annuncio è rivolto ad entrambi i sessi, ai sensi delle leggi 903/77 e 125/91, e a persone di tutte le età e tutte le nazionalità, ai sensi dei decreti legislativi 215/03 e 216/03. Saranno presi in considerazione solo i profili che rispettino i requisiti richiesti. Si prega di inviare dettagliato CV indicando l'autorizzazione alla gestione dei dati secondo Regolamento Europeo UE n. 679/2016.

title: Social Content Creator - Donut Studios (Temporary)
description:
About New Engen

At New Engen, we help brands grow - not just bigger, but smarter. We're a digital marketing agency built to drive meaningful impact across the entire customer journey, bringing together media, creative, influencer, affiliate, and measurement into one connected growth system.

Our in-house creative studio, Donut Studios, produces high-performance, platform-native creative built for modern brands. Our influencer and creator marketing capabilities connect brands to culture and commerce. Across every engagement, our LIFT measurement platform - including LIFT AI - quantifies incremental impact and guides smarter investment decisions.

We're a collaborative group of strategists, creatives, analysts, and makers who are all in on helping brands - and each other - thrive. We work hard, stay humble, and build with purpose.

Learn more about careers at New Engen.

We are committed to equal-employment opportunity regardless of race, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, religion, veteran status, disability, history of disability, or perceived disability.

Social Content Creator - Donut Studios (Temporary)

(Multiple states)

Temporary

Entry Level

WHY DONUT STUDIOS?

At New Engen, we help brands grow, not just bigger, but smarter. We're a digital marketing agency that drives meaningful impact across the entire customer journey by integrating media, creative, analytics, influencer, and retail strategies into performance-driven campaigns.

Donut Studios is our in-house creative studio, purpose-built to craft high-performing, platform-native, and digital content for the world's most ambitious brands. As a key part of New Engen's suite of specialized solutions, alongside offerings like the Acorn Creator Suite - Donut Studios helps unlock breakthrough creative that drives results, fast.

We're a collaborative group of strategists, content creators, copywriters, editors, designers, account managers, and more who are all in on helping brands, and each other, thrive. We work hard, stay humble, and build with purpose.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Are you a hands-on creator who lives and breathes social? We're looking for a Social Content Creator who's equal parts strategist, editor, and producer-ready to lead the charge on organic storytelling for our clients, our brand, and our internal creative initiatives.

In this role, you'll concept, film, and edit content that's platform-native, trend-aware, and built to perform. From TikTok-first campaigns to behind-the-scenes event content, you'll bring big ideas to life-quickly and collaboratively.

Here's what you'll be diving into day-to-day:

  • Own end-to-end short-form content creation-from hook ideation to final edit
  • Draft scripts, outlines, and shot lists based on creative briefs and goals
  • Self-shoot using mobile or light camera setups, with an eye for lighting and sound
  • Edit for multiple platforms with native pacing, captions, and overlays
  • Monitor daily trends, formats, and audio to inspire new concepts
  • Proactively pitch quick-turn content ideas and seasonal moments
  • Deliver polished assets with correct captions, cover frames, metadata, and filenames
  • Collaborate closely with strategy and copy teams to align on brand voice and performance goals
  • Incorporate feedback quickly and iterate toward a high creative bar
  • Keep projects organized with versioning, assets, and file management

WHAT YOU'LL NEED

  • 2-4 years of experience in social content creation, UGC, or short-form production
  • Deep knowledge of TikTok, IG Reels, and Shorts formats-including hook strategies and storytelling structure
  • Comfortable filming yourself and/or directing internal talent on camera
  • Strong editing skills in Adobe Premiere, CapCut, or comparable tools
  • Visual instincts for text overlays, captions, and movement that feel native to the platform
  • A passion for social trends-and the agility to ship same-day ideas
  • Highly organized and collaborative, with the ability to thrive under tight turnarounds
  • An idea-first mindset with a constant flow of bold, unhinged, and culturally relevant concepts
  • A trend-setter mentality with a drive to create and test original formats-not just follow existing trends
  • A distinct sense of humor and personality for crafting engaging content
  • Los Angeles or surrounding area preferred

Bonus points for:

  • Basic design experience (Photoshop or Figma)
  • Familiarity with VO, AI voice tools, or basic audio editing
  • Exposure to social performance metrics and iteration loops
  • Experience managing a content calendar or publishing workflow

HOW WE'LL MEASURE SUCCESS

  • Velocity - Quality concepts shipped per week
  • Fit - Platform-native execution, hook strength, and watch time
  • Impact - Engagement rate, shares/saves, and lift against campaign goals
  • Craft - Edit quality, pacing, and brand alignment
  • Collaboration - Responsiveness and clarity in feedback loops

TEMPORARY ROLE DETAILS

  • Structure: Temporary position with the potential to convert to permanent based on performance and business needs. Initially scoped for temp employment with the possibility of extension.
  • Schedule: Full-time (approx. 40 hours/week), standard business hours
  • Location: Fully remote
  • Equipment: A company laptop will be provided and shipped directly to you
  • Time Off: Accrue sick/safe time at 1 hour per 30 hours worked (up to 80 hours). Unpaid time off may be taken with approval.
  • Compensation: $30-37 hourly, commensurate with experience (no benefits or PTO included)

The range is applicable for the labor market where the role is intended to be hired. Final base salary is directly related to each candidates' qualifications and experience uniquely.

New Engen is proud to have offices in Seattle, New York, and Northwest Arkansas, each one anchoring teams that drive creativity, collaboration, and culture. But great work isn't limited to those zip codes. We also support a distributed team of remote employees across the country, giving people the flexibility to do their best work wherever they are.

We currently hire full-time employees in the following states: Arkansas, California, Colorado, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Michigan, Missouri, Nevada, New Jersey, New York, North Carolina, Oregon, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, Virginia, Washington, and Wisconsin

Let's Build What's Next Together - If you're passionate about media and eager to make a meaningful impact with a team that's all in, we'd love to hear from you.

Important Notice About Job Openings:

Please be aware of fraudulent job postings, consulting engagements, solicitations, or employment offers from suspicious sources. These attempts are often designed to steal your personal information or trick you into paying fees for recruitment or training services.

New Engen does NOT charge any application, processing, or training fees at any stage of the recruitment or hiring process.

All legitimate job openings will be posted directly on our careers page, and all communication about these roles will come exclusively from Recruitment outreach, conducted via email through our ATS, JazzHR, and never through unsolicited text messages or the @newengen.online domain.

If you have any concerns about the authenticity of any message claiming to be from New Engen, please contact us immediately at peopleteam@newengen.com before taking any further action.

title: Creative Team (Art Director & Copywriter)
description:
About R/GA

R/GA is a global digital innovation agency specializing in digital product development, brand transformation, media, and communications for major companies. We are on a mission to help companies grow by enabling them to change-across their business, the experience they offer, and the campaigns that take them to market.

Our work blends what we call Stories and Systems: narrative and design, technology and interface working together. R/GA is a brand, but also a collective of atypical outsiders and misfits who drive everything we do through bold creativity and bright, strategic thinking.

About the Role

R/GA is searching for a creative team - Art Director & Copywriter - who together deliver world-class ideas that push the boundaries of creativity. You're fluent in concepting as a team and collaborating with others. You know how to turn insights into powerful ideas and bring them to life through impeccable craft, whether in words, visuals, or integrated experiences.

You're big on big ideas, comfortable selling them to clients. You're endlessly curious, inventive, and at ease working across formats, channels, and technologies.

Our creative teams think broadly-about culture, behavior, platforms, and emerging tech. They craft brand stories and systems, and connect people with brands through work that's original, unexpected, strategic, and always on brand.

On any given day you might

  • Concept and collaborate as a team (AD + CW) and with other creatives including designers, strategists, creative directors, technologists, and project managers.
  • Develop campaign platforms and translate them into integrated executions across channels-film, social, digital ecosystems, product experiences, activations, content, and more.
  • Craft world-class copy (CW) and visual expressions (AD): from headlines and scripts to design systems, art direction, and layouts.
  • Explore and apply new technologies, from AI tools to emerging platforms, in the service of idea development and execution.
  • Work closely with creative leadership from the NY office to ensure concepts are on brand and strategically grounded.
  • Partner with project managers to ensure deliverables are on time, on budget, and aligned with client expectations.
  • Present ideas to clients at all levels with clarity, conviction, and a strong creative point of view.
  • Guide and mentor junior talent, fostering a collaborative and high-performing creative culture.

The Ideal Team

  • A portfolio demonstrating conceptual strength and the ability to produce innovative, award-winning work across channels.
  • Work that shows outstanding craft—writing craft (CW) and design/visual craft (AD).
  • Curiosity, passion for digital media, and an interest in emerging tech such as AI, platform innovation, and new creative tools.
  • The ability to juggle multiple projects and maintain quality under pressure.
  • Strong collaboration and communication skills—able to accept feedback, iterate, and elevate ideas together.
  • A desire to build brands, solve business problems creatively, and produce work that earns attention and results.
  • Marketing sense and an understanding of how campaign platforms work across systems and touchpoints.

You Bring

  • A portfolio showcasing stories + systems thinking and strong idea-to-execution capabilities.
  • 3 to 6 years of experience working at world-class agencies and/or on global brands.
  • Ability to collaborate seamlessly across departments (strategy, tech, PM, production, media).
  • Fluency with writing tools (CW) and design/execution platforms (AD).
  • Strong ideation and presentation skills that convince, sell, and inspire.
  • Proficiency in English (written & verbal).
  • Academic background in Advertising, Communications, Design, Copywriting, or equivalent experience.

title: Senior Experience Designer
description:
About R/GA

R/GA is an independent creative innovation company built for the intelligence age. We harness the power of design and technology to create more valuable experiences for people and brands.

From reimagining interfaces with AI to launching entirely new platforms and services, we help organisations adapt to what's next and shape what comes after. Our teams combine craft, curiosity and technology to deliver work that drives both business and human impact.

At R/GA, we don't just respond to change - we help shape it.

The Role

This is a craft-driven role for someone who can confidently tackle today's experience design challenges while helping shape the interfaces of the future. It's about designing experiences that are intuitive, intelligent and inclusive, grounded in solid UX principles and open to evolution. Experimentation is essential; we're looking for someone who questions convention, embraces emerging technologies and contributes to defining new standards in an AI-driven world.

We're also looking for people who bring energy and ambition. Designers who care deeply about the craft, get excited by complex problems, and see design as a superpower.

You'll work closely with strategy, technology, creative and client teams to turn insight into innovation. Whether you're mapping a product journey, prototyping a new interaction, or pushing the limits of what AI tools can do, you'll bring a clear perspective on how technology can unlock better experiences for people and brands.

On any given day you might be

  • Lead the UX design on products, services and platforms across a range of industries
  • Explore how AI-powered interactions (e.g. conversational UIs, generative interfaces, smart automation) can deliver more human experiences
  • Design experiences that evolve and adapt to human interactions
  • Use generative technology and other tools to concept, prototype, and test ideas faster and smarter
  • Translate research into clear user flows, wireframes, and design systems
  • Collaborate with visual designers, strategists, technologists and clients to shape the work
  • Facilitate workshops and collaborative working sessions
  • Present and rationalise design decisions to internal teams and external stakeholders
  • Contribute to how we build, lead and evolve design teams at R/GA

You'd be the right fit if you 

  • Are curious about what's next for UX and see AI as a key part of that evolution
  • Have experience launching digital products or services that put people first
  • Bring a clear, structured approach to problem-solving
  • Have the ability to turn creative concepts into clear, actionable design 
  • Think in systems and patterns, not just screens
  • Are comfortable working in an agile, collaborative, fast-moving environment
  • Can clearly articulate your ideas, rationale and ambition
  • Are excited to experiment with AI-powered design tools, and build your own
  • Have a strong sense of craft, care about the details, and take pride in making things better

You bring

  • 4+ years of experience in UX, product, service or interaction design
  • A portfolio that shows thoughtful, real-world design solutions and the thinking behind them
  • Mastery of Figma, Miro, Google Workspace and other prototyping and collaborative tools
  • Strong prototyping skills with the ability to quickly bring ideas to life and explore interactions at different levels of fidelity
  • Hands-on experience with AI tools such as ChatGPT, Midjourney, Runway, ElevenLabs, GitHub Copilot, or your own custom stack
  • A track record of using AI in your design process, whether through prototyping, testing, or building internal tools
  • A solid understanding of interaction design principles, accessibility, and inclusive design
  • A drive to contribute to the culture of a progressive, multidisciplinary team

Diversity

Everyone is welcome at R/GA, and we are committed to empowering our talent to bring their authentic self to work. If you have any requests or requirements to support you throughout the interview process, please let us know.

title: Brand & Community Lead
description:
Pressable is a leading managed WordPress hosting platform trusted by agencies, developers, and businesses with demanding performance and support needs. As part of Automattic, we bring deep WordPress roots and technical excellence to every customer experience.

With strong momentum in the mid-market and agency segments, we're looking for a Brand & Community Lead to define how Pressable shows up in the world → from our visual identity and storytelling to our community presence and activations.

About the Role

This fully remote role is about building brand love and recognition. You'll own the Pressable brand voice, identity, and narrative across every channel, making sure everything we publish, sponsor, and share feels unmistakably "Pressable."

You'll drive awareness, engagement, and advocacy through creative content, community partnerships, and brand activations. That includes our social channels, sponsorships, affiliate and influencer programs, and WordPress community presence-as well as hero content and storytelling that elevate how people perceive our brand.

You'll collaborate closely with Product Marketing, Demand Gen, and Growth to ensure our brand shows up with consistency and intent. You'll bring new ideas for how to expand our presence through events, content formats, channels, and partnerships.

This is a high-visibility, cross-functional role for someone who's equal parts creative and strategic, with the confidence to steward a brand and the curiosity to experiment with new ways of building awareness.

Key Responsibilities

  • Brand Strategy & Identity
    • Own and evolve Pressable's brand voice, tone, and visual expression across all touchpoints.
    • Ensure consistency and quality across messaging, creative, and customer communications.
    • Lead brand storytelling that differentiates Pressable and deepens trust with our audiences.
  • Content & Storytelling
    • Oversee creation of hero brand content and customer stories.
    • Partner with agencies and freelancers to produce content that elevates our brand and community.
    • Use AI tools to accelerate production, repurpose assets, and maintain editorial consistency.
    • Support launches and campaigns with compelling creative and storytelling assets.
  • Community & Advocacy
    • Own Pressable's social media presence and grow engagement across all channels.
    • Lead our involvement in the WordPress community, including events, sponsorships, and collaborations.
    • Support and grow our affiliate and influencer programs with new toolkits and campaigns.
    • Develop advocate and ambassador initiatives to turn customers and partners into storytellers.
  • Brand Activations & Events
    • Oversee our brand presence at conferences, WordCamps, and sponsorships.
    • Create event content, experiences, and campaigns that extend reach before and after each event.
    • Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure consistent messaging and creative execution.
  • Measurement & Optimization
    • Define and track brand KPIs (awareness, engagement, reach, share of voice, sentiment).
    • Evaluate ROI of sponsorships, activations, and campaigns-and refine for impact.
    • Partner with Product Marketing, Demand Gen, and Growth to align brand efforts to business goals.

You Might Be a Fit If You

  • Have 5-7+ years in brand, content, or community marketing-ideally in B2B SaaS and/or the WordPress community.
  • Have owned a brand's voice and enjoyed translating it across diverse formats and channels.
  • Are as comfortable leading creative direction as you are managing execution.
  • Have experience building awareness through social, partnerships, and community involvement.
  • Understand how to measure brand health and connect awareness to engagement and growth.
  • Thrive in fast-moving environments and know how to create leverage through AI and automation.
  • Know how to deploy and orchestrate AI agents to extend your reach, automate routine work, and scale creative output.

Bonus Points

  • Experience working with agencies, developers, or WordPress professionals.
  • Familiarity with the WordPress community and events like WordCamps.
  • Background in creative direction or storytelling.
  • Experience scaling affiliate or influencer programs.
  • Comfortable using AI tools for creative ideation, editing, or workflow automation.

What You'll Work On Initially

  • Refresh Pressable's brand guidelines (voice, tone, and visuals).
  • Relaunch our social media strategy and content cadence.
  • Audit and strengthen our WordPress community presence and sponsorships.
  • Explore new affiliate and ambassador toolkits to amplify reach.
  • Oversee development of new hero content, including brand videos, customer stories, and partner narratives.

Why Join Pressable?You'll have the opportunity to shape the future of a growing product with real impact. You'll work with a small, fast-moving team that values autonomy, clarity, and action → and you'll do it all within the broader Automattic family.

Salary range: $110,000 to $140,000 USD. Please note that while salary ranges are presented here in USD, we will pay in local currency.

 Remote (North America / UK) #LI-Remote

We are searching for high-caliber candidates with the skills and qualities to have a net positive for Automattic. Pay will reflect the potential contribution and the impact you can bring, which may, in some cases, go beyond the range stated for the right candidate.

Perks & Benefits of Joining Pressable!

  • Health Benefits for US-based staff (99% Paid Employee Medical, Dental, and Vision).
  • Matching 401(k) for US-based staff. 
  • Life and Disability Insurance for US-based staff (100% Paid Life, & LTD).
  • Work from home with home office setup and coworking allowances.
  • Open vacation policy (no set number of days per year). 
  • Hardware and software, books or conferences that promote continued learning.

So, are you ready to embark on this thrilling WordPress adventure? We can't wait to welcome you to the Pressable team and empower you to make the web a better place. Come join us in crafting an exceptional customer experience and revolutionizing the world of WordPress hosting. Apply now and let's build something amazing together!

 #LI-Remote

About Automattic 

Now in our 20th year, we're the people behind WordPress.com, WooCommerce, Tumblr, Simplenote, Jetpack, Longreads, Day One, PocketCasts, Beeper, and more. We believe in making the web a better place.

title: Junior Graphic Designer
description:
About the Role

title: Creative Director, Art
description:
About R/GA

R/GA is a global digital innovation agency specializing in digital product development, brand transformation, media, and communications for major companies. We are on a mission to help companies grow by enabling them to change, across their business, the experience they offer and the campaigns that take them to market.

Our work tends to be a fusion of what we call Stories and Systems: the narrative and the design, technology and the interface working together. R/GA is a brand but it's also a collective of atypical outsiders and misfits who drive everything we do through bold creativity and bright, strategic thinking.

About the role

R/GA are on the hunt for a future focused creative leader who can deliver world-class work that pushes the boundaries of creativity and craft. You're fluent in concepting and collaborating, and have mastered the art of visual storytelling across multiple platforms and channels. You're big on big ideas and comfortable selling your work, design thinking and concepts to clients at all levels. You're passionate about growing the next generation of creative talent and confidently lead multidisciplinary teams to do the best work of their careers. You're endlessly curious and inventive. Your concepts and executions are fresh, unexpected, strategic and always on brand.

Our Creative Directors think for themselves, think for others, think for our clients and most importantly, think digitally and visually. Think about how people are interacting with new technology, from mobile devices to AI-powered interfaces and immersive environments, and find new ways for people to experience and shape our brands. You actively use AI as a creative partner - to generate ideas, prototype worlds, visualize concepts with the highest level of craft and push executions further, faster and smarter. Daily requirements can range from high-level thinking like defining visual platforms and campaign ecosystems, to the granular craft behind design systems, motion behaviors and every detail of a digital experience.

Here's what you should know if you want to be a Creative Director at R/GA:

On any given day you might

  • Manage the creative strategy and overall visual direction of client work
  • Ensure the quality of work produced is in line with agency and client expectations
  • Serve as the department leader and ensure the continued growth of the team
  • Manage client relationships and work to deepen their knowledge of powerful marketing communications
  • Identify the conceptual and strategic direction for various projects, integrating design, technology and AI, and clearly present those ideas to the team and the client

The ideal person

  • Is an extremely strong leader who can manage multiple clients and projects
  • Has a nurturing approach to team members and is committed to showcasing their ideas and encouraging progression
  • Has experience building ideas in a digital environment
  • Has a background in art direction and is a fantastic visual storyteller
  • Has ample experience working with and presenting to clients of all levels
  • Has experience working collaboratively with multiple internal stakeholders/teams
  • Has outstanding oral, written, and presentation skills
  • Can look at corporate brands from a fresh angle
  • Has a strong knowledge of interaction Design, technology, usability and experience design

You Bring

  • 8+ years of experience in advertising and/or digital media
  • Bachelor's degree in Advertising, Design, Visual Communication, or equivalent education and experience
  • Proficiency in English (Written & Verbal)
  • Award winning work
  • A combination of digital and traditional creative experience
  • Ability to impact a team's understanding of how to get to great work

Diversity

No matter who you are, where you're from, who you love, follow in faith, disability (or superpower), status, ethnicity or the gender you identify with (please do tell us your pronouns), you're welcome at R/GA. If you have any requests or requirements to support you throughout any interview process, please let us know.

title: Motion & Graphic Designer
description:
About the Role

Kickstart your motion and graphic design career with an internship at Enigma Digital.

3 months

Paid Internship

On-Site, Noida, Sector 62

Position Overview

Are you passionate about visual storytelling, animation, and creating captivating motion content? Enigma Digital is seeking a skilled Motion & Graphic Designer who is eager to bring ideas to life through animation, motion graphics, and visual design. In this internship, you'll have the opportunity to learn from industry experts, contribute directly to client projects, and create visually engaging designs that resonate across digital platforms.

Responsibilities

  • Conceptualize and produce high-quality motion graphics, 2D animations, and visual designs for digital marketing campaigns, social media, websites, and interactive projects.
  • Collaborate closely with senior designers and marketers to create compelling visual stories through motion design.
  • Develop detailed storyboards, style frames, and design assets that align with brand guidelines and project goals.
  • Apply best practices in animation principles, typography, visual composition, and color theory.
  • Stay updated on the latest design trends, motion techniques, and software tools to keep creative output fresh and innovative.
  • Actively contribute to brainstorming sessions and present creative concepts internally.

Requirements

  • A degree (or equivalent practical experience) in Animation, Graphic Design, Visual Communication, Multimedia Arts, or a related field.
  • Proficiency in Adobe After Effects, Premiere Pro, and design tools such as Photoshop and Illustrator.
  • A strong portfolio showcasing expertise in motion graphics, animation principles, and visual storytelling.
  • Passion for digital design, animation, and creative problem-solving.
  • Excellent collaboration skills, strong communication abilities, and a growth mindset.
  • Ability to work effectively under tight deadlines in a fast-paced, creative environment.

Nice-to-Haves

  • Experience with 3D software such as Cinema 4D, Blender, or Maya.
  • Basic knowledge of video editing, compositing, and visual effects.
  • An eye for detail and commitment to producing polished, professional work.

What You'll Gain

  • Practical, hands-on experience creating motion graphics for real-world brands and campaigns.
  • Mentorship and feedback from experienced designers and creative leads.
  • An opportunity to build a strong, diverse portfolio in motion design and visual storytelling.
  • Access to cutting-edge design tools, techniques, and collaborative projects.
  • Potential for full-time career opportunities based on performance and business needs.
  • A dynamic, supportive, and inspiring work environment where creativity thrives.

Why Enigma?

At Enigma, we believe creativity can change the way people experience the world. Our culture celebrates innovation, teamwork, and continuous growth. You'll be part of a passionate, talented community that encourages pushing creative boundaries and producing meaningful work.

Join Enigma and craft the digital stories of the future!

title: Creative Director - Visual & Motion Design
description:

You will partner closely with creative leadership, strategy, clients (internal or external), and production to deliver compelling, on-brand visual experiences.

Your role is critical in elevating the visual voice of the agency, championing innovation in design, and translating big ideas into polished, emotionally engaging visual and animated work.

Location

Mumbai

Responsibilities
