title: Sr. Network Development Engineer, ADC Network Design, Engineering, and Solutions
description:
Description

AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain - and we're looking for talented people who want to help.

You'll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You'll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you'll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

Amazon Web Services Networking is searching for hands-on Senior Network Development Engineer to join our network team that owns critical Amazon Web Services IP networks for our public sector regions. The team drives design, engineering, implementation, operations, reliability, and sustainability of these next-generation networks and works on innovative ways to automate and scale our network as we expand. Position location can be Herndon, VA, Arlington, VA, or Seattle, WA.

Our ideal candidate is hands-on, autonomous, detail oriented, possesses strong written and verbal communication skills, strong project management and time management skills, and has a successful track record of engineering, delivering, and optimizing durable solutions. The desire and ability to work in a fast paced, collaborative environment; with geographically diverse teams is essential. This is a fantastic opportunity to work with some of the best engineers in the industry and help shape the direction of one of the largest and fastest growing cloud networks in the world.

Responsibilities

  • As a Senior Network Development Engineer working in AWS Networking, you will have end to end ownership (design, engineering, implementation, operations escalation) of our current and newly developing IP networks
  • Lead the design and evolution of highly available hyper-scale networks, driving them from concept to launch, and beyond
  • Define and influence strategy within and outside the team
  • Lead project teams to deliver impactful benefits to our customers
  • Work closely with our network engineers and software developers to automate network deployment and operations
  • Drive operational excellence projects to improve network performance and reliability
  • Troubleshoot complex problems and develop innovative solutions
  • Hiring, mentoring and coaching engineers
  • Review and implement changes on the network

Physical and Other Requirements

  • Must be willing to support an off-hours operations escalation pager rotation
  • Experience dealing with customers during problem resolution and operating efficiently under pressure
  • The ability to sit in front of computer during scheduled work hours with appropriate breaks while maintaining a high level of alertness and attention to detail
  • Travel to data center/network sites and Amazon/customer offices as needed

About the team

Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world's most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating - that's why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn't followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don't let it stop you from applying.

We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there's nothing we can't achieve in the cloud.

Here at AWS, it's in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

We're continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth's Best Employer. That's why you'll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Basic Qualifications

  • 8+ years of major internet routing protocols experience
  • 5+ years of working in a Linux/Unix environment experience
  • 3+ years of automation scripting using Python, Bash, Shell and/or Perl experience
  • Experience in IP networking technologies such as - BGP, OSPF, IS-IS, MPLS, TCP, SLB, network hardware platforms, and packet forwarding architectures
  • Network design, engineering, implementation, and/or operations experience
  • Experience leading solutioning, planning, engineering, growth, operations, and/or enhancement of industry leading IP network solutions
  • Bachelor's degree in Networking, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or related technical discipline

Preferred Qualifications

  • Ability to lead and deliver complex network projects and drive operational excellence
  • Proficiency in developing network automation software
  • Ability to drive and manage customer impacting network events
  • Ability to work in a fast paced and collaborative environment
  • Ability to deal with ambiguity, prioritize, and time manage effectively

title: Software Dev Engineer II, Secure Customer Content Storage and Analytics, AWS Applied AI Solution - Core Services
description:
As part of the AWS Applied AI Solutions organization, we have a vision to provide business applications, leveraging Amazon's unique experience and expertise, that are used by millions of companies worldwide to manage day-to-day operations. We will accomplish this by accelerating our customers' businesses through delivery of intuitive and differentiated technology solutions that solve enduring business challenges. We blend vision with curiosity and Amazon's real-world experience to build opinionated, turnkey solutions. Where customers prefer to buy over build, we become their trusted partner with solutions that are no-brainers to buy and easy to use.

Join our Applied AI Solutions team and help build the secure data foundation that powers AWS's AI-driven business applications. We own critical infrastructure including event-based data stores, machine learning training platforms, and data access interfaces that enable responsible AI development while maintaining the highest standards of privacy, security, and compliance. Our platform serves as the backbone for storing, processing, and managing data that fuels AI-powered capabilities across AWS, ensuring customers retain full control over their information.

As a Software Development Engineer II, you'll take ownership of production systems that handle the complete data lifecycle-from ingestion through model training to customer opt-out controls. You'll maintain and refactor existing services for enhanced performance and scalability, while designing new solutions to meet emerging requirements from business application teams. Working alongside scientists and engineers, you'll enable them to build and improve AI models, balancing innovation with compliance requirements.

This role offers exciting opportunities to solve complex challenges around secure data storage, efficient querying at scale, and building self-service platforms that empower development teams. You'll drive the evolution of established platforms to meet future needs, directly impacting how AWS delivers increasingly sophisticated AI-powered capabilities while ensuring customers maintain complete control over their data throughout every stage of processing.

Key job responsibilities

  • Own and deliver complete software features for event-based data storage systems, from design through deployment and operations, ensuring they provide flexible, secure, and compliant platforms for AI-powered business applications
  • Maintain, refactor, and enhance existing storage and analytics platform, identifying opportunities to improve performance, scalability, and operational excellence while eliminating technical debt
  • Design and implement software solutions for data access interfaces and authorization systems within established architectural strategies, seeking guidance from senior engineers when facing complex technical tradeoffs
  • Mentor team members through code reviews, documentation, and knowledge sharing-providing meaningful feedback, training newcomers on system architecture, and facilitating technical discussions that promote operational excellence
  • Resolve operational issues by identifying root causes and implementing permanent fixes for compliance features, data deletion APIs, and customer opt-out mechanisms, going beyond quick workarounds
  • Work directly with business application teams and stakeholders to understand requirements, translating them into technical solutions that balance customer needs with platform sustainability
  • Take ownership of security implementations including encryption, access controls, audit trails, and PII handling, ensuring software can be maintained and extended by other engineers
  • Drive improvements to self-service platforms and control planes, making priority tradeoffs between new feature development and operational work while maintaining governance standards

A day in the life

You'll participate in engineering standups, providing updates on data storage features and resolving platform blockers. Your hands-on work includes refactoring services for performance, implementing secure data interfaces, and ensuring compliance through quality code and testing. You'll collaborate with scientists and engineers, helping them navigate data access patterns and authorization requirements while mentoring teammates through code reviews. When production issues arise, you investigate root causes to maintain reliable data deletion and opt-out mechanisms. Regular syncs with business application teams help you translate evolving requirements into technical solutions that balance innovation with security and compliance, while also documenting features and training teammates on system lifecycles.

We're continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth's Best Employer. That's why you'll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

title: Sr. Content Strategist, Technical Content Experience
description:
Join us on the Content Excellence team as a Senior Content Strategist and help define the standards and frameworks that govern AWS technical content.

At AWS, content consistency doesn't happen by accident; it requires clear specifications for how content is structured, organized, evaluated, and managed. As AI transforms content creation and management, these foundational rules become essential for authors and machines alike.

As part of the Technical Content Experience organization, the Content Excellence team is looking for a strategic thinker to define what good content looks like, and then partner with platform teams, content creators, and AI systems to ensure those standards are understood, adopted, and applied. The right candidate thinks in frameworks and systems and influences through clarity and collaboration. They excel at translating ambiguity into actionable specifications that other teams operationalize.

This role requires exceptional communication skills, strong analytical thinking, and the ability to influence without authority.

Key job responsibilities

  • Define the content ecosystem strategy that outlines what customer needs AWS content must serve, how properties should be organized, and what content should be single-sourced.
  • Create content architecture standards. Define the categories, taxonomy, and organizational structures that enable unified and intelligent content management.
  • Manage the content model. Specify content types, components, and metadata that authors and AI systems use as their foundation.
  • Establish content patterns. Document assembly rules for each content type so anyone can create or generate compliant first drafts.
  • Maintain the Content Design System as the single source of truth for how AWS content should be created and managed.
  • Define quality criteria that distinguishes good content from bad, enabling data-driven evaluation and routing decisions.
  • Specify content lifecycle stages, triggers, and evaluation criteria that enable proactive management at scale.
  • Define gold-standard criteria for AI training corpus curation.
  • Drive alignment across stakeholders on definitions, criteria, and specifications.

About the team

The Content Excellence team ensures every piece of AWS technical content delivers a predictable, high-quality experience aligned to customer needs and business outcomes. We define the standards that make this possible at scale: content ecosystem strategy, content architecture, content models, content patterns, quality definitions, governance criteria, lifecycle frameworks, AI training corpus criteria, and the Content Design System.

Basic Qualifications

  • 5+ years of digital content management, communications, or content strategy experience
  • Experience using data and metrics to determine and drive improvements
  • Experience using content management systems and content tools
  • Experience designing, planning, and delivering content solutions that meet the needs of technical business customers
  • Experience building cross-functional partnerships and influencing stakeholders across the organization to act without having a direct reporting relationship
  • Experience establishing governance frameworks or quality criteria for content at scale

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience building ontologies, taxonomies, and semantic relation frameworks
  • Experience implementing change management successfully transitioning programs into large-scale operations
  • Experience defining requirements for AI-assisted content systems
  • Understanding of content lifecycle management frameworks
  • Experience rationalizing or consolidating fragmented content ecosystems
  • Experience documenting standards, patterns, or guidelines for broad organizational adoption
  • Familiarity with structured content concepts (DITA, component content management, content reuse)

title: Senior ASIC Design Engineer, Hardware Compute Group
description:
Description

Amazon Lab126 is an inventive research and development company that designs and engineers high-profile consumer electronics. Lab126 began in 2004 as a subsidiary of Amazon.com, Inc., originally creating the best-selling Kindle family of products. Since then, we have produced numerous devices like Fire tablets, Fire TV and Amazon Echo. What will you help us create? The Role: As a Senior ASIC Design Engineer, you will be part of an advanced design and architecture team that is creating the semiconductor designs which will power millions of new Amazon devices. You will work closely with multi-disciplinary groups including System Architecture, Architecture, and Software teams, to architect and implement the advanced imaging pipelines that will enable our products to capture the world with stunning clarity.

Key job responsibilities

  • Architect and Design world class imaging hardware
  • Communicate and work with team members across multiple disciplines
  • Develop detailed design specifications and documentation
  • Perform RTL coding and synthesis
  • Work with Partners/Suppliers to optimize and customize their products
  • Run industry standard code quality tools and fix issues found by them
  • Participate in test plan and coverage reviews

The ideal candidate should have experience with RTL development environments and fluency in modern hardware description languages. They should have developed complex IP blocks from scratch that have successfully been integrated in SOCs or other such silicon that have been productized in consumer devices. They should have experience in design, development, and implementation of dedicated hardware for image processing pipelines. They should be familiar with modern SoC architectures, various interconnect topologies such as AMBA AXI, APB, AHB, and implementations. Knowledge of image processing algorithms and experience with multimedia pipeline design is desirable. Experience with I/O interfaces such as SPI, eDP, HDMI, MIPI CSI/DSI/ is also preferred. Experience with memory instantiation and memory compilers is also preferred. We are looking for a self-driven individual who can work with architects, HW and SW developers and can quickly resolve blocking issues.

Basic Qualifications

  • Master's degree or Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering or related field
  • Experience with design of Image Signal Processing (ISP) pipelines and image processing algorithms
  • 5+ years of RTL development experience with a record of taping out production silicon
  • Experience with design development using Verilog/SystemVerilog
  • Experience in defining micro-architecture from architecture guidelines and model analysis
  • Experience in performance/power/area analysis and trade-offs
  • Proficient in design methodologies and EDA tools
  • Experience working with Synthesis, timing closure, and design constraints

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with ARM and various DSP ISA
  • Experience in entire design flow from architecture to final silicon
  • Experience debugging system-level issues
  • Good programming skills in C/C++ and scripting skills in Python, Tcl, and/or Perl
  • 10+ years of practical experience
  • Experience with wide variety of low power design techniques
  • Working experience with high performance industry standard buses like AMBA AXI4
  • Experience in integrating third party IP blocks, building top level modules, defining clock domains and power domains
  • Knowledge of FPGA and emulation platforms
  • Knowledge of SoC architecture
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills

title: Senior UX Researcher, Sponsored Products Market Intelligence
description:
The Sponsored Products and Brands team at Amazon Ads is transforming how millions of customers discover and shop for products through innovative AI technologies. We're reimagining the shopping experience by developing intelligent solutions that help customers find relevant products while enabling brands to connect meaningfully with their audience. Our research-driven approach combines customer behavior analysis, shopping patterns, and advanced AI to create more intuitive and personalized advertising experiences. We're committed to understanding shopper needs and preferences, ensuring our AI technologies enhance the customer journey while delivering value to advertisers. Join us in building shopping experiences that put customers first while advancing the future of digital advertising.

Key job responsibilities

  • Lead complex, high-impact quantitative research initiatives globally, establishing large-scale programs that track longitudinal trends, customer sentiment, and behavioral patterns across US, EU, and emerging markets.
  • Design and execute sophisticated quantitative studies using advanced statistical methods, and experimental design to evaluate new features, measure their impact, then develop metrics that improve shopper experience.
  • Drive research innovation by integrating AI technologies throughout the research process, leveraging automation to optimize research workflows and accelerate insight generation.
  • Partner with AI/ML teams to translate data-driven insights into model improvements, using statistical validation, and qualitative anecdotes to optimize performance across Product, Engineering, and Business initiatives.
  • Lead the socialization of quantitative insights, supplemented by targeted qualitative research when needed, presenting data-driven findings to senior leadership and serve as a subject matter expert in UX research methodologies.

A day in the life

As a Senior UX Researcher on our Sponsored Products team, you'll spend your days transforming large-scale survey data into actionable insights that shape how millions of shoppers discover and engage with ads.

A typical day might involve analyzing responses from thousands of global customers, designing multi-market perception studies, or launching studies to measure the impact of new ad experiences. You'll regularly collaborate with data scientists and engineers, helping translate customer sentiment and preference data into model improvements. When not deep in statistical analysis, you'll be presenting your findings to product leaders, turning complex metrics into compelling stories that drive product strategy.

While your foundation is in quantitative research methods, you know when to supplement with targeted qualitative research to add context to your findings. If you're energized by solving complex problems with data, passionate about the intersection of AI and user research, and want to directly impact how millions of customers interact with Amazon's ads, we want you on our team.

About the team

The Sponsored Products Marketplace Intelligence team within Sponsored Products and Brands is responsible for ranking, placing and pricing billions of ads per day. We seek a Senior UX Researcher to lead initiatives focused on improving the shopper experience through innovations ranging from training AI models, influencing ML modeling to designing new interactive discovery experiences. In this role, you'll have an opportunity to deliver outsized business impact and delight hundreds of millions of customers.

title: Senior Product Manager Tech, Global Subscription Growth, Music Growth and Marketing
description:
We are looking for a Senior Product Manager - Tech to own the subscription internationalization and intelligent chatbot strategy for Amazon Music. This role puts you at the forefront of expanding Amazon Music's subscription business in high-growth international markets, while also defining a new global standard for how customers interact with and manage their subscriptions.

In this role, you'll sit at the intersection of music streaming, payments, promotions, and conversational AI. You will be responsible for making Amazon Music subscriptions both globally scalable and locally relevant, while also creating a smart, intuitive chatbot experience that empowers customers to engage with their subscription directly - from plan exploration and upgrades to cancellations, entitlements, and benefits access.

You are highly data-driven, proactive, and comfortable navigating ambiguity across business and technical domains. You thrive on working backwards from diverse customer needs worldwide and scaling mechanisms that work across regions and use cases.

Key job responsibilities

Key Workstreams

1. Internationalization & Market Expansion

  • Launch and scale Amazon Music subscription offers in new and growing international markets.
  • Localize pricing strategies, promotions, and upsell experiences to meet regional customer expectations and regulatory requirements.

  • Build scalable mechanisms for subscription onboarding, lifecycle management, and retention globally.

2. Intelligent Subscription Chatbot

  • Define and drive the global strategy for Amazon Music's subscription chatbot.
  • Create an AI-driven conversational interface that helps customers with subscription-related needs, including plan discovery, pricing questions, upgrades/downgrades, cancellations, entitlements, and benefit access.
  • Collaborate with Engineering, Data Science, and CX teams to design personalized, proactive flows that reduce friction and increase customer confidence in managing their subscriptions.
  • Continuously improve the chatbot's accuracy, coverage, and customer delight using data insights and generative AI capabilities.

About the team

The Music Subscriptions team builds and maintains the systems that power Amazon Music's global subscription business. We address challenges that affect millions of customers daily, spanning international expansion, payment integrations, promotions, upsells, and next-generation subscription experiences.

title: Brand and Social Lead, Amazon Music
description:
Amazon Music Japan is seeking a strategic, insight-driven Brand and Social Lead to define how Amazon Music shows up in Japan's cultural and competitive landscape. This role owns Japan's brand narrative foundations and social strategy, while directly leading priority brand initiatives and integrated campaigns.

As a senior individual contributor, you will set strategic direction and personally own high-impact brand projects end-to-end - from local customer insight and brief development through launch and post-mortem. You will operate hands-on in shaping campaign direction, reviewing creative, and guiding execution to ensure cohesive storytelling, cultural relevance, and measurable business impact across campaigns and tentpole moments.

Key job responsibilities

  • Define and evolve Japan's brand narrative architecture, translating global brand direction into locally resonant strategy grounded in market and competitive insight
  • Lead the development and execution of integrated brand campaigns across tentpoles, partnerships, and content launches, ensuring cohesive storytelling across touchpoints
  • Own end-to-end social strategy across priority platforms, including operating model, content pillars, and channel roles aligned with business objectives
  • Oversee creative, media, and social agency partners to ensure high-quality execution, brand consistency, and timely delivery
  • Bring a social-first perspective to integrated marketing initiatives and collaborate on amplification planning
  • Navigate global and local stakeholder alignment, including facilitating leadership reviews and cross-functional planning forums
  • Represent Japan brand strategy in global discussions and ensure effective local implementation
  • Define success metrics for brand and social initiatives, conduct structured post-campaign analysis, and translate insights into actionable improvements

A day in the life

You will operate at the intersection of brand strategy and campaign execution. A typical week may include shaping the narrative for upcoming campaigns, aligning cross-functional stakeholders on direction, and facilitating leadership reviews to drive clarity and decisions.

You will review creative concepts and social plans with agency partners, ensure high-quality execution across channels, and analyze performance trends to inform continuous improvement. This role balances long-term brand roadmap development with hands-on ownership of priority initiatives, translating strategy into measurable in-market impact.

Success requires comfort navigating ambiguity, influencing diverse stakeholders, and moving fluidly between strategic thinking and operational delivery.

About the team

Amazon Music is an immersive audio entertainment service that deepens connections between fans, artists, and creators. From personalized music playlists to exclusive podcasts, concert livestreams to artist merch, Amazon Music is innovating at some of the most exciting intersections of music and culture. We offer experiences that serve all listeners with our different tiers of service: Prime members get access to all the music in shuffle mode, and top podcasts, included with their membership; customers can upgrade to Amazon Music Unlimited for unlimited, on-demand access to 100 million songs, including millions in HD, Ultra HD, spatial audio, and Audiobooks from Audible; and anyone can listen for free by downloading the Amazon Music app or via Alexa-enabled devices. Join us for the opportunity to influence how Amazon Music engages fans, artists, and creators on a global scale.

Basic Qualifications

  • Speak, write, and read fluently in English
  • Speak, write, and read fluently in Japanese
  • 8+ years of experience in brand strategy, integrated marketing, or social leadership roles
  • 5+ years of experience leading end-to-end brand campaigns at an agency or client-side marketing organization
  • Demonstrated ownership of brand narrative, positioning, and campaign strategy with measurable in-market impact
  • Experience building and scaling multi-channel social strategy aligned to business objectives
  • Experience in brand campaign briefing, creative development, and production processes
  • Experience influencing and aligning cross-functional and global stakeholders at senior levels
  • Strong analytical capability connecting brand activity to business performance signals
  • Experience in Japan's media landscape, platform dynamics, and cultural context

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience building and managing financial models for business forecasting and problem solving, or experience in building financial and operational reports/data sets that inform business decision-making
  • Experience in subscription, media, entertainment, or digital consumer businesses
  • Experience leading high-visibility tentpole campaigns or cultural launches
  • Experience managing creative, media, and social agencies in Japan
  • Familiarity with brand health tracking, performance measurement frameworks, and full-funnel marketing models
  • Experience navigating senior leadership reviews and driving decision clarity in ambiguous environments

title: Senior Mechanical Engineer - Spacecraft Design, Bus Structures
description:
Amazon Leo is an initiative to increase global broadband access through a constellation of 3,236 satellites in low Earth orbit (LEO). Its mission is to bring fast, affordable broadband to unserved and underserved communities around the world. Project Kuiper will help close the digital divide by delivering fast, affordable broadband to a wide range of customers, including consumers, businesses, government agencies, and other organizations operating in places without reliable connectivity.

Export Control Requirement: Due to applicable export control laws and regulations, candidates must be a U.S. citizen or national, U.S. permanent resident (i.e., current Green Card holder), or lawfully admitted into the U.S. as a refugee or granted asylum.

We're looking for a Senior Structural/Mechanical Engineer with a background in space vehicle designs and electro-mechanical systems integration. The ideal candidate has experience making major optimization trades for satellite bus architectures including integration with payload subsystems. This is a hands-on role for a highly collaborative individual that combines superb technical and analytical capabilities to convert analytic trade studies to a qualified physical vehicle, while optimizing for high volume production and meeting cross discipline requirements. The candidate will design, analyze, and test satellite primary and secondary structures while ensuring all requirements are met.

Responsibilities

  • Perform mechanical design and layout trade studies for satellite primary structure contributing to overall system architecture including inputs and requirements from cross discipline teams - Electrical, RF, Thermal, Dynamics, Mechanisms, Materials, Manufacturing, Assembly, and Test.
  • Generate detailed designs via CAD systems and engineering drawings with appropriate geometric dimensioning and tolerancing (GD&T).
  • Conduct or oversee static, dynamic and modal structural analysis via finite element methods as needed to support design work.
  • Deliver an efficient structural design capable of high volume production throughput (DFMA).
  • Documentation and presentation of materials for technical design reviews.
  • Design and support test campaigns, both development and qualification/acceptance, for space hardware.

Basic Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree in Structural or Mechanical Engineering or related discipline, or equivalent experience.
  • 6+ years of experience including significant time designing structural systems for aerospace.
  • 4+ years of experience with structural materials and processes including metals, composites, bonded and bolted joints.
  • 3+ years of experience in structural sizing including classical methods (hand calculations) and Finite Element Analysis (FEA) of primary and secondary structures.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Advance degree (Master's or PhD) in structural or mechanical engineering or related discipline.
  • CAD expertise (Siemens NX or equivalent, NX Teamcenter, GD&T per ASME y14.5).
  • Finite element analysis skills - (FEMAP/NASTRAN or equivalent).
  • Experience supporting development and qualification test campaigns of space/flight hardware.
  • Experience with common spacecraft subsystems: Power Systems (Solar Arrays, Batteries), Electric propulsion system integration, Secondary structures, Avionics assemblies, harnessing, ADCS elements and placement implications, Payload subsystems integration, Deployables, Release mechanisms, Moving mechanical assemblies, Thermal control systems and sizing (materials, finishes, MLI, HPAs, radiators), LV Interfaces.

title: Senior UX Designer, Amazon Delivery CX
description:
Description

Have you ever experienced the delight of knowing you'll get a delivery exactly when and how you wanted it? Amazon's Customer Experiences team (ADX CX) is seeking our next Senior UX Designer to build on this delight and reimagine how millions of customers engage with their purchases after checkout. In this role, you'll explore new ways for shoppers to understand and manage their orders, working directly with senior leaders and multi-disciplinary teams to connect evolving customer needs with emerging technologies. Your work will focus on crafting and driving innovative features that adapt to evolving expectations for convenience, control, and confidence in post-purchase experiences. By uncovering insights and crafting intuitive solutions, you'll help re-shape how people think about and interact with their orders - from the moment they complete a purchase until it arrives and what happens next - turning these all into moments customers can look forward to in shopping.

Responsibilities

  • Bring forward innovative ideas and solutions and raise the bar on customer's experience with delivery

title: Sr. Thermal Design Engineer, Amazon LEO
description:
Amazon Leo is Amazon's low Earth orbit satellite network. Our mission is to deliver fast, reliable internet connectivity to customers beyond the reach of existing networks. From individual households to schools, hospitals, businesses, and government agencies, Amazon Leo will serve people and organizations operating in locations without reliable connectivity.

Export Control Requirement: Due to applicable export control laws and regulations, candidates must be a U.S. citizen or national, U.S. permanent resident (i.e., current Green Card holder), or lawfully admitted into the U.S. as a refugee or granted asylum.

We are looking for a Sr. Thermal Design Engineer who combines strong technical, research and analytical capabilities with a demonstrated ability to get the right work done quickly and effectively. The successful candidate must have experience working on active thermal control of space vehicles, producing top notch thermal analysis and providing essential thermal related input and recommendations to system architects and subsystem leads.

Key job responsibilities

  • Support extensive architectural trades with preliminary thermal analysis on spacecraft and subsystems for orbital operational conditions and vehicle control options.
  • Perform detailed and comprehensive analyses of active thermal control systems, including all required hardware and corresponding tradeoffs.
  • Drive design optimization for performance, mass and cost, while meeting system reliability requirements through application of a range of thermal control hardware options.
  • Define, lead and execute thermal cycling and thermal vacuum tests for development and qualification testing at subsystem and vehicle levels.
  • Support the procurement department in sourcing strategy for all required components.
  • Define and select test equipment as needed (e.g., TVAC and thermal chambers, sensors, data logging equipment).
  • Conduct testing of prototypes and engineering models for fast design feedback loops.
  • Develop and execute reliability plan for demonstration of lifetime of active components.
  • Document trades and design evolution, and present results during design reviews.

Basic Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree in mechanical engineering or equivalent
  • 5+ years experience with thermal control of spacecraft, and delivering solutions optimized for mass, cost, reliability, performance, and manufacturability.
  • 3+ years of experience with thermal simulation software (e.g., Thermal Desktop (SINDA), Ansys), and programming (e.g., python, Matlab).
  • 3+ years experience with test planning, test set-up, results correlation and system verification for production.
  • 3+ years experience in development and/or selection of passive and active thermal control hardware.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Advanced degree (Master's or PhD) in mechanical engineering or related field.
  • Experience developing hardware and spacecraft that have been to space, and which successfully operated as intended.
  • Developing spacecraft thermal solutions for high power flux components.
  • CAD skills (NX or Solidworks)
  • Experience with interpreting and correlating data from on-orbit telemetry.

title: Thermal Design Engineer, Amazon Leo (Satellites)
description:
Amazon Leo is Amazon's low Earth orbit satellite network. Our mission is to deliver fast, reliable internet connectivity to customers beyond the reach of existing networks. From individual households to schools, hospitals, businesses, and government agencies, Amazon Leo will serve people and organizations operating in locations without reliable connectivity.

Export Control Requirement: Due to applicable export control laws and regulations, candidates must be a U.S. citizen or national, U.S. permanent resident (i.e., current Green Card holder), or lawfully admitted into the U.S. as a refugee or granted asylum.

We're looking for a Thermal Design Engineer who combines superb technical, research and analytical capabilities with a demonstrated ability to get the right work done quickly and effectively. This person must have experience working in spacecraft design, producing top notch thermal analysis and providing essential thermal related input and recommendations to vehicle architects and subsystem leads.

Key job responsibilities

  • Support extensive architectural trades with preliminary thermal analysis on spacecraft and subsystems for orbital operational conditions and vehicle control options.
  • Perform from conceptual to detailed analysis of Bus and Payload subsystems, components and mechanisms.
  • Develop automated scripts enabling rapid analysis updates accommodating trades (power states, duty cycles, structural mapping).
  • Design, analyze and select heat pipes as needed for bus and payload components.
  • Define and execute test setups for thermal cycling and TVAC tests for development and qualification testing at subsystem and vehicle level, and correlate test results with mission operations models.
  • Define and select test equipment as needed (e.g., sensors, data logging equipment etc.)
  • Perform thermal model correlation using test results.
  • Conduct testing of prototypes and engineering models for fast design feedback loops.
  • Plan and execute characterization, qualification and acceptance tests.
  • Document trades and design evolution, and preparation of materials for design review gates.
  • Support thermal design for terrestrial equipment and systems with CFD analysis.

About the team

Amazon Leo's Thermal Team is responsible for the thermal design solutions for all components in Amazon Leo's satellites and customer terminals. We work together with many engineering disciplines (e.g., mechanical, power, electrical, network, manufacturing) to find the design that maximizes performance while minimizing mass, cost and manufacturing complexity, and enabling all components to operate reliably over the satellite's target lifetime. The team follows all hardware components from conceptual design, detailed analysis, development testing, through launch and flight.

Basic Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree in mechanical engineering or related discipline, or equivalent experience.
  • 3+ years of experience with thermal analysis software, including writing custom scripts to enable rapid design trades and iterations for system and subsystem optimizations for various use cases.
  • 1+ year experience with test planning, test set-up, results correlation and system verification for production.
  • 1+ year of experience in development of, and/or selection of, range of thermal control hardware, materials and finishes, paints, interface materials, MLI blankets, heat-pipes, radiators, temperature sensors, and or heaters.
  • 1+ year experience with thermal simulation software (e.g., Thermal Desktop (SINDA), Ansys), and programming (e.g., python, Matlab).

Preferred Qualifications

  • Advanced degree (Master's or PhD) in mechanical engineering or related field.
  • Experience with spacecraft thermal design, and delivering design solution optimized for mass, cost, reliability, performance, manufacturability.
  • Experience developing hardware and spacecraft that have been to space, and which successfully operated as intended.
  • Developing spacecraft thermal solutions for high power flux components.
  • CAD skills (NX or Solidworks)
  • Experience with interpreting and correlating data from on-orbit telemetry.

title: German Localization Editor, Amazon Devices and Services
description:
The Amazon Devices Localization team is seeking a German Localization Editor passionate about language, quality, and customer experience. As part of our Editorial team, you will be responsible for the linguistic quality of German content across the Amazon Devices portfolio, including Alexa, Fire TV, Kindle, and much more.

This role is for someone who obsesses over details, thrives in ambiguity, and brings strong linguistic expertise paired with a builder's mindset. You'll work at the intersection of content, tech, and global customer experience, helping our German-speaking customers understand, enjoy and engage with our products.

Key job responsibilities

  • Own end-to-end linguistic quality for German content across device software, marketing, and packaging.
  • Develop, evolve, and evangelize style guides, terminology, and editorial standards for German.
  • Provide guidance and feedback to external vendors and internal stakeholders to ensure scalable, high-quality localization.
  • Partner with localization program managers, localization & QA engineers, and development teams to ensure linguistic quality and launch readiness.
  • Proactively identify localization issues and customer-facing risks, engage with corporate and in-country partner teams to drive solutions, and escalate where needed.
  • Analyze customer feedback, analytics, and team data to continually improve our German content experience.
  • Contribute to cross-language editorial initiatives that improve consistency, efficiency, and impact at scale.

A day in the life

You'll work closely with fellow editors, program managers, and software and QA engineers to shape the Amazon Devices customer experience in German. Your day may include localizing high-visibility content for Alexa or Fire TV, or marketing copy for the next product detail page on our website, leading vendor meetings, running linguistic quality reviews, updating the German style guide, or validating terminology for an unreleased device. You'll be part of a collaborative team that thrives on quality, clarity, and customer obsession. We move quickly when it matters, operate at global scale, and continuously raise the bar for localized content that resonates with customers.

About the team

Every day, we invent on behalf of our customers, partners, and communities. We are focused on delivering a constellation of devices, services, and experiences that empower our customers to stay connected, safe, informed, productive, educated, and entertained. Our customers inspire us, and they've been at the heart of how we invent and evolve our products.

Our global team of about 40 editors, engineers, program managers, and QA experts collaborates across time zones and product lines to deliver experiences that feel natural to every customer: we don't just translate, we craft the voice of Amazon Devices for each marketplace, helping shape how customers live, connect, and interact with technology in their own language.

Basic Qualifications

  • Native-level fluency in German
  • Bachelor's degree in translation, localization, linguistics, or technical communications with a focus on German, or comparable fields
  • Professional experience in German software localization, translation, or writing/editing experience
  • Proficiency with CAT tools, TMS and terminology platforms (e.g., memoQ, QTerm, SDL Studio, Passolo)
  • Ability to work cross-functionally
  • Proven ability to manage deadlines, adapt to shifting priorities, and operate with a bias for action

Preferred Qualifications

  • Master's degree in translation, localization, linguistics, or computational linguistics
  • Experience working in a multilingual editorial or localization team
  • Interest in language technology, LLMs, or AI-assisted content workflows/quality validation

title: Principal TPM, Music Subscription and Identity Org
description:
Music Subscription and Identity org acts as the backbone of Amazon Music by powering the identity, subscription, and payments systems that underpin growth, retention, and profitability. We undertake Amazon-wide initiatives which are foundational to Music's long-term growth.

Identity org will unify customer identity across Amazon, reshaping how customers and households share content and benefits. For Music, this ensures seamless access across Alexa, Prime, and AMU. We will deliver child and teen profiles that are compliant, safe, and trusted.

Subscription org will support Music's launch in new countries and expand our acquisition funnels improving payment parity and building efficient purchase flows. We will deepen our lifecycle strategy, ensuring customers find value from their first play through last mile. Personalization will improve early-use stickiness, cancellation flows will evolve into benefit-rich retention moments, and payments will become more resilient to prevent involuntary churn.

We are seeking an experienced Principal Technical Program Manager to lead complex, cross-functional initiatives that drive significant business impact. This role requires a strategic leader who can navigate ambiguity, influence senior stakeholders, and deliver results at scale across multiple organizations. You will be trusted to operate with complete independence and are often assigned to focus on areas where the business and/or architectural strategy has not yet been defined.

You effectively partner with all levels of leadership. You are adept at building consensus. You are able to represent, verbally and in writing, complex decisions, technical trade-offs, and potential solutions clearly to leaders up to 3 levels above to help them understand the decisions they have to make and what options they have. You influence organization priorities, system owners, business, and technology direction. You manage cross-functional communication to ensure all stakeholders are informed and needs aligned. You recognize when current programs are susceptible to prior technology failure patterns and actively steer teams to avoid repeating these failures. You know when a design or solution requires additional technical guidance (e.g., from senior engineers or subject matter experts). You show excellent judgment in driving executive-level escalations. You deliver solutions that meet Amazon high standards for customer satisfaction, efficiency, stability, extensibility, simplicity, and operational excellence.

About the team

From personalized music playlists to exclusive podcasts, concert livestreams to artist merch, Amazon Music is innovating at some of the most exciting intersections of music and culture. title: Brand Specialist, AVS (Amazon Vendor Services)
description:
Description

Are you a self-starter with a passion for e-commerce, looking to expand your retail skillset and shape the way the biggest brands work with Amazon?

As Brand Specialist, you will be part of the Amazon Vendor Services (AVS) team, which provides a suite of services designed to help Amazon's most strategic brands working across all key areas of retail business. You will work with vendors supplying the top brands on Amazon, acting as the main point of contact for them. Your priority will be to work backwards from the customer, identifying inputs to grow your brands and improve their performance. You will do this while facilitating great business relationships and ensuring vendors' satisfaction with the service.

Key responsibilities as a Brand Specialist are:

  • Own the relationship end-to-end with top brands
  • Partner internally with key retail functions to contribute to the definition of priorities and operational goals according to the brands' needs
  • Partner with our Media Team to ensure best-in-class event execution
  • Define joint business plans and assist in delivering impact for the brands you support directly
  • Regularly audit metrics to continually drive quality of the AVS experience and deliver targets
  • Contribute to continuous enhancement and innovation within the AVS across MENA

Brand Specialists work on a variety of tasks for the brands they support, typically falling within the five focus areas. These include but are not limited to:

  • Business Advice: Providing business advice based on deep financial analysis and reporting, to identify areas of opportunities
  • Selection: Helping grow the selection of products by managing the launch of new products and improving product discoverability
  • Availability: Support driving process improvement and operational excellence within our vendor community by reducing costs, defects, and lead times, while increasing innovation
  • Traffic: Analyzing traffic channels and working on initiatives to generate traffic by supporting the development and execution of marketing and merchandising campaigns
  • Conversion: Planning and executing promotional activities and improving customer journey on Amazon

Alongside this, you will contribute to improving the efficiency of the service itself. We are constantly working to build scale for this service through the right set of tools and cross-functional collaboration including but not limited to Tech teams.

This role is available in Dubai, United Arab Emirates.

This role offers 360° exposure to different areas of the industry e-commerce, opening a multitude of career paths.

Key job responsibilities

  • Be the point of brand and category expertise for Amazon Tier 1 Vendors - serving as a trusted business advisor
  • Help by innovating new solutions to complex business challenges
  • Own and manage significant pool of Tier 1 Vendors
  • Developing, selling, implementing, and evaluating business plans to deliver or exceed on the vendor improvement plans
  • Collaborating with multi-functional teams (Marketing, Finance, Supply, Operations, among others)
  • Managing and improving pricing, visibility, and availability for your brands
  • Translate Category Business Plans into tangible action plans
  • Develop productive working relationships with the vendors and create the mutually beneficial value
  • Turn raw data and insights into actionable conclusions
  • Use analytical tools and integrate various data systems to develop new insights
  • Identify opportunities to improve our products, services, processes, systems, and tools
  • Work with different parts of the Amazon business to implement improvement projects, including working with category teams, product teams, and technical support team

    • Basic Qualifications

    • Bachelor's degree in business, Science, Engineering fields or Supply Chain with an excellent academic background
    • Between 1 - 4 years of experience in key accounts management, projects management, customer facing roles or commercial roles
    • Excellent analytical, problem solving and negotiating skills
    • Advanced MS Office Skills (Excel proficiency is a must)
    • MNCs experience especially in FMCG/E-commerce/Digital is preferred.
    • English proficiency: Arabic speaking is an advantage

    Preferred Qualifications

    • Experience in process improvement
    • Experience managing large amounts of data
    • Experience using analytical specific tools such as Google Analytics, SQL or HTML

title: Safety Design and Engineering Manager
description:
About the Role

At Amazon, we're working to be the most customer-centric company on earth. To get there, we need exceptionally talented, bright, and driven people. One focus area of the Amazon Workplace Health & Safety (WHS) mission is to provide tools, resources, and environments that support safe, efficient, and healthy behaviors and encourage employees to proactively manage their health and well-being.

To support this focus area, Amazon is seeking WHS Design and Engineering Managers to play a critical role to ensure high standards of safety are built into systems and processes across new and legacy Fulfilment Centres, Sort Centres and Delivery Stations across Europe.

As the single-threaded leader for safety integration, the successful candidate will partner with Design, Engineering, Construction, Startup, Launch, and Operations teams to ensure safe and regulatory-compliant facilities and equipment are delivered to our operational teams. Success in this role requires the ability to comprehend and apply complex technical documents, requirements, and solutions while communicating clearly and consistently, both verbally and in writing.

You will leverage strong safety and technical expertise when collaborating with cross-functional stakeholders, building the trust and confidence essential to driving safety excellence across the network.

Location: Luxembourg, with up to 75% of time traveling within EU countries and the UK.

Responsibilities

  • Drive Safety Innovation: Function as a catalyst, communicator, facilitator, researcher, and technical thinker to assess and enhance facility and equipment system designs from a WHS perspective, ensuring proper execution throughout the project lifecycle.
  • Ensure Compliance: Through effective collaboration and positive stakeholder relationships, ensure safe implementation and regulatory compliance for all activities related to site expansion, launch, acquisition, divestment, and equipment system deployment.
  • Lead Technical Reviews: Serve as WHS technical voice during design reviews and on-site assessments, ensuring compliance with Amazon standards and regulatory requirements. Lead safety technical reviews and risk assessments for new equipment systems.
  • Conduct Site Assessments: Verify all applicable regulatory requirements, engineering specifications, and WHS standards are integrated into construction, installation, and start-up phases. Create and maintain punch lists, distribute reports to relevant teams, and track mitigation actions to reduce risks to acceptable levels before project launch.
  • Demonstrate Ownership: Exhibit the knowledge, ownership, and self-direction necessary to drive change in an autonomous technical environment.
  • Optimize Travel Efficiency: Plan and arrange travel with frugality and time efficiency while aligning with critical project milestones and stakeholder needs.
  • Capture Lessons Learned: Collect, categorize, and share insights from completed projects to ensure continuous improvement and incorporation into future launches.

Qualifications

  • A Bachelor degree or equivalent level of qualification in line with the European Qualifications Framework (EQF)
  • Demonstrated understanding and application of risk assessment methodologies
  • Experience in responsibilities in safety and/or environmental programs in manufacturing, production, engineering/facilities or service operations
  • Experience implementing lean principles and process improvement in an operational environment
  • Relevant program or project management experience
  • Experience using data and metrics to determine and drive improvements
  • Experience owning program strategy, end to end delivery, and communicating results to senior leadership
  • Proficient in use of Microsoft Office and other computer-based tools
  • Valid driving license with experience driving in the UK and Continental Europe

Preferred Qualifications

  • NEBOSH Diploma level or equivalent qualification
  • Technical knowledge of EU machinery safety standards and regulations, robotics and automated conveyors to meet all European country's requirements and lead procurement and implementation of systems across the network
  • Technical knowledge and project experience in building construction and fire safety
  • Strong planning and prioritization capabilities with demonstrated ability to manage multiple competing priorities in dynamic environments

title: Lead ASIC Design Verification Engineer, Amazon Leo
description:
Description

Amazon Leo is Amazon's low Earth orbit satellite network. Our mission is to deliver fast, reliable internet connectivity to customers beyond the reach of existing networks. From individual households to schools, hospitals, businesses, and government agencies, Amazon Leo will serve people and organizations operating in locations without reliable connectivity.

The Role

Be part of Amazon Leo's sub-team responsible for defining and implementing the digital chip SOCs for communications via Low Earth Orbit satellites and Amazon gateways. This is a unique opportunity to define a groundbreaking wireless solution with few legacy constraints. The team works with customer requirements and wireless system teams to define modems, high-speed interfaces, embedded processors, and DSP solutions in latest CMOS generation technologies.

In this role you will

  • Implement a state of the art verification environment to facilitate testing of the RTL against reference Matlab/C models
  • Develop detailed test plans and write tests, run regressions, collect coverage matrices and report progress to the program
  • Write tests in C/C++ to execute on embedded CPU
  • Develop tests for FPGA and emulation platforms
  • Run formal verification of complex blocks to ensure functional correctness
  • Work with the design and communication systems team and participate in system level verification using test benches constructed using UVM, System C and DPI-C
  • Develop a highly automated environment to run regressions that can be used to make builds and maintain organization of the database

Export Control Requirement

Due to applicable export control laws and regulations, candidates must be a U.S. citizen or national, U.S. permanent resident (i.e., current Green Card holder), or lawfully admitted into the U.S. as a refugee or granted asylum.

Basic Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree in Electrical or Communications Engineering or a related field
  • 7+ years in verification preferably in communication systems
  • 5+ years in UVM, C/C++ and scripting

Preferred Qualifications

  • Master's degree or Ph.D. degree in Electrical Engineering or related field
  • 10+ years in digital verification, preferably in communication systems
  • Familiarity with Matlab
  • Familiarity with formal verification techniques
  • Strong written and verbal skills

title: Sr. Industrial Designer, WHS Global Mobility
description:
The Workplace Health and Safety Global Mobility (WGM) team ensures associate safety through prevention-focused design in Amazon's new buildings.

We specialize in three critical operational areas: Powered Industrial Truck (PIT) operations, Mobile Elevating Work Platform (MEWP) systems, and Autonomous Guided Vehicles (AGV) integration. This role directly impacts associate safety by implementing safety standards at the design phase, reducing operational risks through preventive measures, maintaining operational efficiency while prioritizing safety, and creating standardized safety protocols across global operations.

You will collaborate with design engineering teams to develop and implement safety standards that protect associates while optimizing building efficiency. Your work ensures that new Amazon facilities incorporate safety considerations from the ground up, rather than retrofitting solutions later. The role focuses on prevention through design initiatives, risk assessment and mitigation strategies, global safety standard development, cross-functional team collaboration, and operational efficiency optimization.

This position is integral to maintaining Amazon's commitment to workplace safety while supporting operational excellence. You will drive strategic safety initiatives that shape the future of our facilities, ensuring that safety considerations are built into every aspect of our building designs. Through your expertise, you will help create safer, more efficient workspace for Amazon associates worldwide.

Key job responsibilities

  • Lead prevention-through-design initiatives for mobile equipment safety across worldwide operations. This includes:

title: Sr Mechanical Engineer, TIPM, Global Realty Building Design & Engineering
description:
Amazon is currently looking to hire an experienced Mechanical Engineer to join our team as Senior Technical Infrastructure Program Manager (TIPM), playing a pivotal role in leading design, facility, and engineering activities within Amazon's Fulfillment Network across Europe. This role will be based in Luxembourg, Spain or the UK, with periodic travel required. Reporting directly to the EU Head of Global Realty Building Design and Engineering, the successful candidate will be responsible for developing and cross-functionally managing multifaceted mechanical engineering programs aimed at optimizing and enhancing the efficiency of both existing and new facilities.

You will be our Subject Matter Expert for mechanical systems, supporting MEP solutions for prototype templates and working with other Global Realty teams such as Sustainability and RME, and Execution teams including Preconstruction and Construction. Collaboration with various stakeholders will be essential to drive the development and management of mechanical design specifications and support the Enterprise Building Automation System being deployed across the worldwide network.

Responsibilities

  • The Sr Mechanical Engineer will be the single-threaded leader on mechanical design initiatives across the EU region while:
  • Define, document, communicate and own mechanical and plumbing engineering design standards.
  • Maintain basis of design (BOD) for mechanical and plumbing disciplines.
  • Tracking key metrics and performance
  • Functioning as consulting SME to stakeholders
  • Building business cases and marketing building improvements
  • Working across the industry to understand trends and develop scalable technologies
  • Supporting the implementation of processes and programs that drive cost optimization in building operations
  • Leading and supporting pilot projects to test innovative mechanical solutions
  • Own prototype equipment specifications and approval for non-prototype equipment.
  • Develop and execute technical goals related to mechanical and plumbing cost reduction, continuous improvement, and sustainability.
  • Develop, cultivate, and manage relationships with third-party engineering consultants. Track and manage quality and cost of consultant work product.
  • Work cross functionally with other Amazon physical retail business groups to share and develop best practices and lessons learned.
  • Maintain commissioning standards in partnership with internal Maintenance teams.
  • Manage projects and effectively influence and communicate with internal and external business partners, contractors and vendors.

About the team

Global Realty Building Design & Engineering (GRBD&E) is the centralized, Architectural and Engineering subject matter expert for Global Customer Fulfillment (GCF), Amazon Transportation Services (ATS), and Global Delivery Services (GDS). The primary functions of GBD are to create and maintain facility design templates, criteria, and guidelines, and centralize facility construction standards, and global design references for output of frugality and the highest standards for those templates. The team creates value for the Operations Network by aggregating business and cross-functional requirements to drive cost control, operational functionality efficiency, consistency, and safety to facilitate rapid decision making.

Basic Qualifications

  • Bachelor Degree in Mechanical Engineering
  • Experience in the field of engineering in commercial/retail buildings
  • Knowledge of Building, Mechanical, Electrical, Plumbing and Refrigeration Codes
  • Ability to manage high level meetings and discussions with technical and non-technical participants
  • Strong project management experience
  • Excellent written and oral communication skills
  • Ability to meet tight deadlines and prioritize workloads
  • Strong organizational skills

Preferred Qualifications

  • Industrial design experience
  • Masters degree in Mechanical Engineering
  • Revit design experience
  • Knowledge of current sustainability trends and practices
  • Experience in use of CFD tools
  • Work experience within field execution, i.e. with General Contractors
  • Project management certification (PMP or equivalent)
  • Construction contract and designer contract legal experience, Blue Book, FIDIC, Code law contracts
  • Cost Management or Quantity Surveying experience
  • Professional fluency in English and speaking of other EU language(s)

title: UX Designer II, RBS Brand Experience (BX)
description:
About the Team

RBS Brand Experience (BX) enables worldwide brands (FBA Sellers and Retail Vendors) with strategic, actionable insights and recommendations through brands-facing solutions, self-help tools, and updated policies to empower them to take a customer backwards approach that drives business growth while reducing the Cost to Serve for both Amazon and Brands. We do this by: 1) identifying and validating underserved Brand pain points, 2) building proof of concept, solution and capabilities in partnership with Brands, 3) scaling the solution by leveraging tech, 4) launching full scale brand facing solutions to WW brands, and 5) continuously improve solutions through control mechanisms (such as; monitoring impact, user base, adoption, GTM).

About the Role

Amazon's Brand Experience (BX) team is seeking an exceptional Senior UX Designer to transform complex brand challenges into intuitive, scalable solutions that drive seller and vendor success. In this role, you will lead user research initiatives, conduct discovery workshops, and translate insights into compelling design solutions. You will collaborate closely with product management and engineering to design wireframes, user flows, and high-quality UX solutions. You will influence the product roadmap and ensure our services are highly usable for both builders and non-technical customers, while revamping existing product experiences to create richer, more engaging interactions for users.

Responsibilities

  • Lead comprehensive user research initiatives including user interviews, usability testing, and competitive analysis to inform design decisions and validate solutions
  • Plan and facilitate customer discovery sessions, workshops, and feedback loops to deeply understand user needs, pain points, and behaviors
  • Synthesize research findings into actionable insights that drive product strategy and design direction
  • Lead the end-to-end delivery of simplified solutions for the best outcome, present a variety of those solutions, and represent the benefits and challenges with each approach
  • Handle complex problems/efforts, decisions, and escalations as well as determine where to simplify or extend solutions for the best outcome
  • Mitigate long-term risks, balance short- vs. long-term needs, and operate effectively at both strategic and tactical levels
  • Lead org reviews and critiques, and craft/present key narratives (e.g., 6-pagers, business reviews, PR/FAQs, OP1/OP2 inputs) to leadership and cross-functional teams to drive focus and outcomes
  • Influence product and engineering decisions by bringing the customer perspective, guiding design choices, and driving long-term impact
  • Set the standard for design excellence while driving clarity in ambiguous initiatives where problems, strategies, requirements, or technical feasibility are undefined
  • Advise partner teams and senior leaders on org goals and design strategy, influencing engineering decisions to enable UX solutions and driving outcomes across teams and geographies

Qualifications

  • 5 to 7 years of experience as a user experience designer, interaction designer, information architect, or similar role
  • An online portfolio or samples of work demonstrating experience creating great user-centered design solutions is a requirement for application
  • Communication and collaboration skills with proven experience partnering with developers to implement designs
  • Demonstrated ability to thrive in fast-paced, ambiguous environments by multitasking, driving design reviews to closure, and collaborating effectively across organizational boundaries
  • Fluency in best practices for web-based information architecture and design, as well as knowledge of usability principles and techniques
  • Proficient in web design considerations, with a background in design/HCI (or equivalent experience) and expertise in tools like Photoshop, Illustrator, Fireworks, and Visio
  • Skilled in creating interactive prototypes with design aesthetics and a focus on simplicity over complexity

Preferred Qualifications

  • 2+ years of mass-market consumer web / mobile products experience
  • Experience working in a collaborative team and working directly with developers for implementation of designs

title: Business Analyst, Global Content
description:
About this role

We're looking for a Global Content Business Analyst to be the analytical backbone connecting content strategy to measurable outcomes-building self-serve dashboards, driving executive reporting cadence, and partnering with BI, product, and tech teams to make data-driven decisions happen fast. This role demands someone who can analyze complex performance drivers, present confidently to senior leadership, manage cross-functional metrics projects, and leverage AI automation to advance efficiency. You'll be our centralized Content analytics expert, responding rapidly to leadership requests while establishing the capabilities, discipline, and cadence that integrate analytics into content planning and investment decisions.

About the team

The Global Content Strategy & Enablement team develops and helps execute Audible's global content vision by bringing objective insights, domain expertise, and intellectual honesty to strategic decisions. As the connective tissue within the Content org and missionaries of Content's vision across Audible, we provide frameworks to enable Content teams to move quickly and effectively, driving cross-functional collaboration and delivering the best experiences for creators and listeners everywhere.

Responsibilities

  • Understand how the Content team's strategy is translated into action and then measured most effectively
  • Build the capabilities for how we integrate analytics into content planning and investments
  • Analyze content business performance and clearly articulate drivers through narrative-driven documents
  • Represent the needs of the Content team and be the centralized Content contact and collaborator with our BI partners to create self-serve dashboards and improve data availability
  • Drive the cadence and readiness for senior-leadership data reporting and facilitate regular reviews
  • Respond rapidly to ad-hoc leadership requests for information
  • Manage metrics-related cross-functional projects
  • Advance Content's efficiency and aims through automation and AI tools
  • Partner effectively with our data, product, and tech partners to "articulate the possible and move fast to make it real"

Requirements

  • 3+ years of experience with Excel (including VBA, pivot tables, array functions, power pivots, etc.) and data visualization tools such as Tableau
  • Experience with one or more analytics visualization tools (e.g., Excel, Tableau, QuickSight, MicroStrategy, PowerBI)
  • Experience in project management including communication, planning, and documentation
  • Experience defining requirements and using data and metrics to draw business insights
  • Experience making business recommendations and influencing stakeholders
  • 1+ years of SQL experience
  • Experience presenting to executives and all levels of leadership
  • Experience communicating complex data insights to non-technical stakeholders
  • Experience collaborating with cross-functional teams and senior leadership

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience using AI tools to automate data reporting or data analysis
  • Experience manipulating data sets, summarizing into metrics, and presenting analysis
  • Experience determining reporting cadence and mechanisms for complex, global organizations
  • MBA, PMP, or related degree or certification
