Required Qualifications

  • 2+ years of professional experience as a visual effects artist in games or related industries
  • Experience working Unity, Unreal, or equivalent game engine VFX tools

Desired Qualifications

  • Experience developing Unreal Blueprints
  • Experience programming in C++ and UnrealScript

As a part of the application process, candidates will be expected to provide a portfolio or demo reel of their work and may be asked to complete an art test.

For this role, you'll find success through craft expertise and a collaborative spirit that prioritizes the delight of players. We will look at your past studies and experience, but for this role, we also look for dedicated people with a personal relationship with games. If you embody player empathy and care about players' experiences, this is the role for you!

Our Perks

Riot focuses on work/life balance, shown by our open paid time off policy and other perks such as flexible work schedules. We offer medical, dental, and life insurance, parental leave for you, your spouse/domestic partner, and children, and a 401k with company match. Check out our benefits pages for more information.

At Riot Games, we put players first. That mission drives every decision in our quest to create games and experiences that make it better to be a player. Whether you're working directly on a new player-facing experience or you're supporting the company as a whole, everyone at Riot is part of our mission. And just like in our games, we're better when we work together. Our goal is to create collaborative teams where you are empowered to bring your unique perspective every day. If that sounds like the kind of place you want to work, we're looking forward to your application.

It's our policy to provide equal employment opportunity for all applicants and members of Riot Games, Inc. Riot Games makes reasonable accommodations for handicapped and disabled Rioters and does not unlawfully discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, handicap, veteran status, marital status, criminal history, or any other category protected by applicable federal and state law. We consider for employment all qualified applicants, including those with criminal histories, in a manner consistent with applicable federal, state and local law, including the California Fair Chance Act, the City of Los Angeles Fair Chance Ordinance for Hiring Ordinance, the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, and the Washington Fair Chance Act.

Per the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance, the following core duties may create a basis for disqualifying candidates with relevant criminal histories:

  • Safeguarding confidential and sensitive Company data
  • Communication with others, including Rioters and third parties such as vendors, and/or players, including minors
  • Accessing Company assets, secure digital systems, and networks
  • Ensuring a safe interactive environment for players and other Rioters

These duties are directly related to essential operations, safety, trust, and compliance obligations within our organization. Please note that job duties may evolve based on business needs and additional responsibilities may be assigned as necessary to maintain operational efficiency and security. 

  • (Los Angeles Only) Base salary range between $109,200.00 - $153,300.00 USD + incentive compensation + equity + 401K with company match + medical, dental, vision, and life insurance + short and long-term disability + open PTO.

title: VO Designer III (Contract)
description:
Riot Games was founded on the principles that putting players first leads to exceptional experiences. Since the launch of League of Legends in 2009, millions of players around the world log in each month to connect through our games and the characters that bring them to life.

As we continue to expand our global content portfolio, we're seeking a VO Designer III (Contract) based in Singapore to support voice productions across Riot's products and select partnerships.

That's where you come in.

The VO Designer III guides the creative, technical, and production processes required to deliver high-quality VO content for Riot experiences. This hands-on role supports a small team of voice designers, collaborates with cross-disciplinary partners, and ensures VO assets reinforce character identity, narrative clarity, and an exceptional player experience. This is a craft supervision role focused on voice design.

Responsibilities

  • Support Audio leads on voice design productions by assisting with scoping, coordination, and execution across VO workflows.
  • Support established voice design direction by ensuring VO clarity, feasibility, and creative alignment across character, gameplay, and skin content.
  • Represent voice design in kickoff and alignment meetings, sharing relevant creative context and direction.
  • Provide clear, actionable feedback on VO work from internal and external partners.
  • Assist in identifying VO opportunities, risks, and constraints, and align with Voice and Audio stakeholders on scope before production commitments.
  • Collaboration with Production & Craft leads to clarify requirements and voice design implications.
  • Support VO asset delivery, localization preparation, and technical implementation in Wwise and the game engine.
  • Help troubleshoot VO-related issues during integration and playtest cycles.
  • Support external or outsourced voice designers through day-to-day guidance and review.
  • Review vendor deliverables to ensure quality, consistency, and alignment with voice design expectations.
  • Perform hands-on VO polish or support work when required.
  • Help uphold creative and technical quality standards for VO assets.
  • Validate technical specs and support in-engine verification.
  • Follow and support established VO workflows, documentation, and delivery practices.

Requirements

  • 5+ years of experience developing audio for games, including shipped titles.
  • Strong experience with voice-over production, processing tools, and techniques.
  • Solid understanding of end-to-end game audio pipelines, including implementation and player experience considerations.
  • Hands-on experience with game engines and Wwise audio middleware.
  • Proficiency with standard audio creation tools (DAWs and common audio plug-ins).
  • Ability to provide clear direction and timely, actionable feedback.
  • Strong communication skills, with the ability to convey complex ideas clearly in English.

Desired Qualifications

  • Familiarity with League of Legends and its characters, products, and player community.
  • Experience mentoring or providing craft guidance to other designers.
  • Experience directing professional voice actors in recording sessions.
  • Familiarity with designing and maintaining dialogue systems and workflows within live-service development pipelines.
  • Experience with advanced or specialized audio tools (e.g., Reaktor, synthesizers, or audio scripting).
  • Familiarity with scripting or audio tooling (e.g., Lua or audio efficiency tools).
  • Wwise certification or equivalent hands-on experience.
  • Mandarin language proficiency is a plus.

Employment Type: employment contract with Adecco, a professional personnel company in Singapore.

Please note that Riot Games does not offer relocation packages for this position. You need to be able to work legally in Singapore, without Riot Games' sponsorship, to be considered

title: Sr Manager, FP&A - Motion Picture Group
description:
About the Role

Sony Pictures Entertainment (SPE) is seeking a Senior Manager of Divisional Finance for the Motion Picture Group in Culver City, CA. This role involves driving strategic planning, financial forecasting, and analytics to support film operations. The position requires technical expertise, leadership, and strong communication skills.

Responsibilities

  • Supervise and assist in preparing annual budgets, forecasts, and mid-range plans
  • Collaborate with teams on film model updates, profitability, and performance tracking
  • Coordinate with various business units on product/content planning and cash flow assumptions
  • Run impairment risk analyses and prepare schedules for auditors
  • Oversee 3-year cash flow and balance sheet forecasts, prepare executive presentations
  • Perform box office and industry analysis, execute advanced modeling techniques
  • Evaluate revenue and cost items, including content valuations and revenue recognition
  • Assist in drafting SOX controls and ensure compliance with financial controls
  • Coordinate with participations group and technical accounting on deal structures
  • Partner with financial reporting for performance comparisons and system enhancements

Requirements

  • BS/BA in accounting, finance, or economics; CPA and/or MBA preferred
  • 5+ years in financial planning, modeling, and accounting, preferably in entertainment/media
  • 1-2 years managerial experience leading teams
  • Expertise in financial modeling, cash flow forecasting, and balance sheet analysis
  • Proficiency in Excel, PowerPoint; SAP or similar ERP experience preferred
  • Experience with enterprise planning systems and BI tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau)
  • Knowledge of IFRS and U.S. GAAP; SOX compliance experience
  • Strong communication skills for presenting to executives
  • Collaborative leadership and process improvement skills
  • Attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities

Additional Information

The salary range is $125,000-$155,000, potentially including incentives and benefits. Actual salary depends on experience, education, and location. This role is hybrid, based in Culver City, CA.

title: Art Producer, mo.co
description:
We are looking for an Art Producer to join the mo.co team in Helsinki to help us deliver amazing game content for our players.

As an Art Producer, you'll be at the heart of our content production, working closely with artists and other disciplines to deliver high-quality game content across every update. You'll manage the entire content pipeline - from concept through implementation - ensuring that everything comes together at the right time and characters, environments, VFX, animations, and all other art assets are delivered smoothly, efficiently, and to an exceptional standard.

You'll be joining a team that communicates openly, makes decisions together, and looks out for one another when things get busy. We don't overcomplicate the process, and we don't let egos get in the way. It's a steady, supportive environment where everyone's here to make the most incredible art and content for our players.

What You'll Be Doing

  • Oversee and manage the full content production pipeline, including scheduling, task prioritization, milestone setting, and progress tracking
  • Maintain clear, consistent communication within the content team and across all relevant internal and external stakeholders
  • Handle all aspects of outsource management from contracts, budgets and invoices to partner communication and the overall relationship
  • Collaborate with other disciplines to define realistic deadlines, align on content needs, and allocate art resources effectively
  • Troubleshoot and resolve art or content-related issues in partnership with the development team
  • Identify and implement strategies that improve efficiency, clarity, and workflow within the content production process

What You Have

  • Professional experience in art/content production, ideally within the mobile games industry
  • Strong understanding of game art creation and content design principles
  • Excellent organizational and project management skills
  • Strong communication abilities and leadership mindset
  • Passion for mobile games and a drive to deliver high-quality, player-facing content

Would Be Nice if You Also Have

  • Experience coordinating multiple art disciplines such as character art, environment art, VFX and animation
  • Familiarity with live-service pipelines and update cadences
  • Knowledge of tools commonly used in game art/content workflows (e.g., Maya, Blender, Photoshop)
  • Experience collaborating with external partners or outsourcing vendors
  • Appreciation for player experience and how art/content contributes to gameplay clarity and quality

Where You'll Be

This role is based in our Helsinki office. The mo.co team works primarily onsite, with flexibility for hybrid work.

title: Senior Brand & Project Lead (w/m/d) - 20-30h
description:
We are a growing boutique agency focused on strategic brand building. Our emphasis is on service providers, personal brands, and visionary entrepreneurs aiming to become the first choice for their ideal clients.

We are seeking an experienced individual to take operational responsibility, with the potential to develop into a Creative Director role.

You will combine strategy with design, guide clients confidently through processes, and ensure excellent quality in design and implementation.

You will work closely with our brand strategist, lead projects and presentations independently, and manage the operational team.

Mid-term, you will assume overall creative responsibility for our brand projects and help shape the company's creative direction.

Responsibilities

  • Lead and moderate client meetings
  • Present brand and design concepts
  • Manage and steer projects independently
  • Proactively communicate and manage expectations

  • Develop and present strategic design concepts
  • Contribute to shaping brand identities
  • Maintain high creative quality standards
  • Coordinate closely with designers and web developers

  • Manage designers and freelancers
  • Plan projects, manage timelines and resources
  • Develop internal processes
  • Ensure strategic consistency across all touchpoints

Requirements

  • 6+ years experience in branding, design, or agency environment
  • Experience leading projects independently
  • Confidence in presentations and client discussions
  • Strong conceptual skills in brand development
  • Excellent design understanding (corporate design, visual systems, digital presence)
  • Structured, solution-oriented working style
  • High standards for quality and clarity
  • Enjoyment of responsibility and entrepreneurial thinking

Ideally, you have worked independently or led projects fully on your own.

What we offer

  • Flexible scheduling: your working hours are results-oriented
  • Remote or hybrid work: work from anywhere, or visit our office in Vienna's 6th district (matcha latte, coffee, drinks included). Mandatory registration in Austria required.
  • Challenging branding projects with business relevance
  • Room for co-creation and development
  • Potential to grow into a Creative Director role
  • Regular team events in Vienna
  • Access to industry events and talks
  • Respectful and appreciative team culture
  • Casual work atmosphere in a small, motivated team

We are looking for a personality with leadership potential, not a traditional employee, to help develop our strategic boutique agency.

The minimum gross salary according to collective agreement for full-time is between €3,330.20 and €4,166.30. Overpayment is possible based on qualifications.

Start date: mid-March 2026

We look forward to your application and the possibility of meeting you soon.

We promote diversity and inclusion, and do not discriminate based on gender, origin, skin color, or sexual orientation. Everyone is welcome.

title: Game Designer, Clash Royale
description:
We are seeking a passionate and experienced Game Designer to join our Shanghai Studio, with a focus on enhancing the gameplay of Clash Royale. You will be expected to independently plan your work, develop design solutions, and implement them using our tools. The ideal candidate will also collaborate with a team of talented developers to solve large-scale challenges.

At Supercell, we believe that small, well-coordinated teams have the potential to create truly memorable games. It's essential that every team member takes pride in their work and understands the responsibility of delivering quality and fun to our players.

As a Game Designer at Supercell, you will work closely with other designers, programmers, and artists to create engaging gameplay content, game modes, and systems that make Clash Royale the best strategy game in the mobile market. You will also be a key contributor in shaping the future of Clash Royale's gameplay, as well as the overall product direction.

If this sounds like you, we'd love to hear from you!

What You'll Be Doing

  • Create and improve gameplay systems/content for Clash Royale.
  • Polish and upgrade core combat design of Clash Royale.
  • Collaborate with other disciplines and teams to produce a cohesive game experience.
  • Support the development of the game with any additional skills.

What You Have

  • Extensive understanding, skills, and experience with gameplay design.
  • Strong player empathy.
  • Understanding other design areas outside of gameplay design is a big plus.
  • Experience using game engine is a big plus.
  • Excellent communication, interpersonal and organizational skills.
  • Absolute passion for playing and making games.
  • Fluent in English - reading, writing and speaking.
  • Being a great teammate - we believe making games is a team sport.

Where You'll Be

This role is based in our Shanghai office. If you are not local, our relocation team would be excited to help you with your move to Shanghai, China. We provide comprehensive support not only for you, but also for your family!

title: Coordinator, Sales Planning & Operations - Global Formats & Brand Licensing
description:
About the Role

We have an exciting new opportunity to support our Global Formats and Brand Licensing team based in London. The formats team is focused on growing the reach of our Unscripted and Scripted format product, such as Who Wants To Be A Millionaire, Dragons' Den and Raid the Cage; by launching and maintaining local adaptations in as many territories as possible. The Brand Licensing team extends the licensing of our IP across consumer products, mobile games, live events and digital/social media channels.

Responsibilities

  • Source all Brand Licensing revenue contracts and amendments and save them in the central SharePoint repository
  • Enter all Brand Licensing revenue deals into S2F and process all unbilled revenue, in collaboration with the local Finance team
  • Update project tracker and communicate progress to all key stakeholders
  • Create process documentation in conjunction with EMEA Central Sales Planning team to document new workflow and deal entry process for all Brand Licensing revenue deals
  • Design reports to ensure VP, Brand Management and Interactives Strategy is kept up to date on the status of all deals

Deal Management & Administration

  • Set up and maintain deals in Sales & Rights Management systems (RPM) and (S2F - Sales to Finance)
  • Manage contractual obligations and ensure all deadlines are met as detailed in the contracts
  • Assist in deal execution, working closely with Sales and Legal on contract drafting, documentation, and execution.
  • Track the workflow of contract, amendment, notification processes for every deal, client schedules, availability lists and ad hoc reports
  • Liaise with Finance on customer checks, invoicing/payment issues, and audit queries
  • Liaise with clients to chase outstanding reports
  • Ensure all Sales Planning Procedures are upheld by seeking appropriate reviews and Approvals
  • Work closely with Sales and Planning in the territory to ensure clear information flow between local and central teams

Process Optimisation

  • Act as key point of contact for Formats and Brand Licensing deal management, guiding Sales planning processes to Sales and Planning teams.
  • Ensure process documentation for formats and Brand Licensing deals entry is kept up to date.
  • Build relationships with LATAM and APAC teams to ensure they are across the current processes and are ensuring systems reflect the latest deals and forecasts.

Reporting

  • Ownership of regular and ad hoc reporting for Sales, providing clear visibility on performance, pipeline, and opportunities.
  • Work closely with Senior Manager to prepare Global Formats budget presentation, including data consolidation, analysis, and clear commercial commentary.
  • Ongoing maintenance and development of the Print Opportunity Database.

Forecasting & Planning

  • Maintain/update forecasts in RPM; process changes in a timely manner and keep Senior Manager and Sales Teams informed of updates regularly
  • Assist with variance analysis on product, deals and/or market assumptions to support Sales in preparing the forecast
  • Proactive follow-up during year-end and other critical periods to ensure deals, forecasts, and budget reports are accurate and complete.

Requirements

  • Educated to a minimum undergraduate degree level preferable (but not necessarily)
  • Ideally, 1-2 years' experience in a similar environment
  • Strong IT skills, in particular Excel (Pivot Tables & Formulae), and must be comfortable in learning new systems
  • Fluency in English is essential
  • Highly numerate with excellent attention to detail
  • Problem Solving & Decision making - demonstrates an ability to address root causes of problems, identify alternatives and propose solutions.
  • Able to use complex information to provide summaries with clear and meaningful insights
  • Managing priorities and competing deadlines during busy periods
  • Proactive and adaptable - anticipate needs in different situations and take appropriate action with guidance and on own initiative
  • Excellent communication skills
  • Team player who is willing and able to assist colleagues

How we take care of you

  • Competitive salary, with annual bonus eligibility.
  • A choice of comprehensive health plan options that fit your lifestyle, including private medical insurance.
  • Rest and recharge during a week off during the winter holidays, in addition to the 25 days of paid annual leave.
  • Participate in extensive learning & development opportunities at all levels, including curated instructor-led classes and high-impact online resources.
  • Build your community by joining our Employee Business Resource Groups, and/or Sony Pictures Action - our racial equity and inclusion strategy.
  • Access to an employee online store filled with a variety of discounted Sony products.
  • Watch the newest movies and TV shows at our exclusive employee screenings at work.
  • Entitlement to apply for an interest-free season ticket loan.
  • Automatic enrolment in the Company's Pension Plan
  • Free counselling and advisory service.
  • You will be automatically enrolled in the Company's Life Insurance and Group Income Protection Plans, subject to any applicable service requirements.
  • The opportunity to attend various social events in our state-of-the-art offices in Paddington.
  • In addition to the above, we have a range of other optional discounted benefits that are reviewed regularly (e.g., gym memberships, travel insurance, activity passes, car leasing, experience days and many more!)

If you require any reasonable adjustments with any part of the recruitment process, including the application or interview process, please contact us atuk_-_peopleandorganisation@spe.sony.com.Please put Reasonable Adjustment Request in the subject line of the email.

Sony Pictures does not allow audio recording, video recording or use of AI note-taking tools during interviews. Candidates requiring these tools as an accommodation during an interview should submit a reasonable adjustment request at the point they are invited to interview.

Sony Pictures Entertainment is committed to equal opportunity in all its employment practices, policies and procedures. No worker or potential worker will therefore receive less favourable treatment due to their race, age, creed, sexual orientation, colour, nationality, ethnic origin, disability, religion, gender, marital status or Trade Union membership (if applicable).

title: Senior 3D Character Artist, Brawl Stars
description:
all positions

Helsinki - onsite - FullTime

Senior 3D Character Artist, Brawl Stars

We are looking for an experienced Senior 3D Character Artist to join Brawl Stars to oversee the development and delivery of Brawlers and skins.

As a Senior 3D Character Artist, you will be at the heart of our art production, working closely with our Art Director, Animators and other artists in the team. Your main responsibility would be to create and oversee the 3D skin production for our brawlers together with our outsourcing partners. Due to the high amount of content for each update, your work would mainly consist of providing clear direction and feedback to our external teams. The ideal candidate should have proven experience in creating high-quality 3D characters, covering the entire process from modeling to implementation within an engine. All in all, we're looking for a strong communicator who takes ownership, is passionate about our games, and is continuously striving for better performance.

We pride ourselves on the level of thoughtfulness, quality, and focus put into every aspect of our work. Be it the games themselves or how we present them to our players - we are passionate about not compromising on quality ever and choosing a long-term approach to make Supercell a truly global games company that will last for many decades to come.

What You'll Be Doing

  • Create and oversee the production of high-quality, stylized 3D character models for Brawl Stars
  • Provide clear, constructive feedback and guidance to external artists and studios
  • Collaborate with the Technical Artists to integrate final characters into the game engine and troubleshooting any technical issues that arise
  • Ensure character models are optimized for performance on mobile platforms without compromising visual quality
  • Maintaining 3D asset libraries and documentation
  • Continuously striving to improve workflows, pipelines and ways to work

What You Have

  • Experience as a 3D Artist in the gaming industry
  • Experience with stylized characters optimized for mobile performance
  • Experience in managing external studios or contractors
  • Must be a clear and concise communicator, able to deliver creative feedback and actionable guidance to multiple external artists
  • Excellent proficiency in Maya, including technical knowledge
  • Strong understanding of composition, color theory, and visual storytelling
  • Passion for mobile games and a drive to create visually appealing content

Where You'll Be

This role is based in our Helsinki Studio.

title: Senior Game Designer, Project R.I.S.E
description:
We are looking for a passionate and experienced game designer who specializes in Unreal Engine to join our Shanghai Studio.

You will be working on one of our new projects. You should be able to independently design new features and systems, and support their implementation, but the ideal candidate will have demonstrable experience in crafting second-to-second action gameplay, including, but not limited to level mechanics, layout design and enemy/AI behavior.

What You'll Be Doing

  • Prototype gameplay content (mechanics, enemies, level interactivity) by yourself with minimal dependencies on other disciplines.
  • On-hands design and iteration of new features, game mechanics and gameplay content
  • Collaborate with other disciplines to follow your designs from initial documentation through to final in-engine polish.
  • Support the development of the game with any additional skills.

What You Have

  • At least 7 years of professional experience as a Gameplay Designer with a portfolio demonstrating combat gameplay and/or level content created by yourself using Unreal Engine & Blueprint.
  • Proven ability to implement enemies, NPCs and combat mechanics into interesting encounters
  • Proactive doer who takes responsibility and pride in their own work when given the freedom to work independently

  • Being a great teammate - we believe making games is a team sport

  • Familiarity with level design tools and concepts. Proven track record of building level blockout.
  • Experience with combat AI and familiarity with Unreal Behavior Trees.
  • Extensive knowledge of Unreal's GAS - from Gameplay Effects to Ability Modifiers

Where You'll Be

This role is based in our Shanghai office. If you are not local, our relocation team would be excited to help you with your move to Shanghai, China. We provide comprehensive support not only for you, but also for your family!

title: Senior Product Designer, Supercell Store
description:
In this role, you will define and shape the user experience of Supercell Store, Supercell ID and other player facing services across all Supercell games. You will be responsible for designing best-in-class user experience for our products and shipping them with our development teams in a very hands-on manner.

Our players are at the center of our product strategy: we believe that an amazing player experience is the key to our success. We work relentlessly to understand what our players need and deliver that in close collaboration with our game teams, other internal teams and external partners.

This is an unique opportunity to work on some of the most recognizable mobile games in the world and to shape our cross-game experience in a highly impactful way.

What You'll Be Doing

  • Work on creating and refining the player experience in Supercell Store and other Supercell products across all Supercell games.
  • Understand what our players want and need, and serve them in the best possible ways.
  • Create outstanding designs with a focus on visual excellence.
  • Work on establishing a roadmap, then work hands-on developing and shipping the products to millions of users, in collaboration with our internal teams as well as external partners.

What You Have

  • Extensive prior experience in product design, including ability to produce visually polished experiences. Please submit a portfolio that reflects your ability to deliver stunning outcomes that make consumers excited.
  • Prior experience from either large-scale consumer services, large-scale e-commerce services or free-to-play mobile games is required.
  • Demonstrated ability to drive results in a collaborative environment across many autonomous cross-functional teams and functions, including product development, operations, and marketing.
  • Proactive doer with an attitude to work on what's most impactful, including striking the right balance between the short-term and the long-term.
  • Strong commercial judgment with ability to combine qualitative and quantitative data points to make data-informed decisions.

Where You'll Be

This role is based in our Helsinki Studio. The Supercell Store team members are primarily located in our Helsinki studio but as a global business you will have key stakeholders in other locations.

title: Product Designer (Formless)
description:
About the Role

As Formless expands beyond simple lead capture into more advanced sales qualification and research interviews, we are looking for a craft strong individual contributor to help bring clarity, consistency, and trust to these new experiences.

You will shape full journey experiences for agent driven products, strengthen edge cases, and align design decisions with real technical constraints and phased roadmaps. Your work will help Sales Agent deliver efficient lead capture, qualification, and handoff, and help Research Agent produce credible, repeatable qualitative insights.

This is where you will come in.

You will help turn emerging agent concepts into dependable, scalable experiences that teams and customers can trust, while shaping the design foundations that future use cases will build on.

Responsibilities

  • Design clear and trustworthy agent experiences across Sales and Research use cases
  • Pair closely with a Senior Product Designer to align on intent and shared patterns
  • Own execution from specs to prototyping and design reviews
  • Keep product managers and engineers closely aligned on scope and feasibility
  • Systemize journeys and interaction patterns as Formless scales into new scenarios
  • Identify and harden edge cases so experiences feel reliable and predictable
  • Use and contribute to the Echo design system to create scalable and accessible patterns

  • Strong experience working closely with product managers and engineers in cross functional teams
  • Specialism in zero to one product development, not only feature improvements

  • Strong visual and interaction design skills, with a focus on clarity and usability
  • Hands on experience conducting user research, usability testing, and iterating based on feedback

  • Fluency in English

title: Technical Artist, mo.co
description:
All positions

Helsinki - onsite - FullTime

Technical Artist, mo.co

We are looking for an experienced Technical Artist to join the mo.co team in Helsinki to help us deliver amazing & high performing visuals for our players.

As a Technical Artist, you'll bridge the gap between art and technology - designing efficient workflows, crafting shaders, and ensuring our art assets look and perform beautifully in-game. You'll collaborate closely with artists, developers, and technical partners to make sure the team works seamlessly across creative and technical disciplines. Your work will shape both the visual fidelity and the production efficiency of our game.

You'll be joining a team that communicates openly, makes decisions together, and looks out for one another when things get busy. We don't overcomplicate the process, and we don't let egos get in the way. It's a steady, supportive environment where everyone's here to make the most incredible art and cosmetics for our players.

What You'll Be Doing

  • Act as a bridge between artists and developers, finding creative solutions that balance artistic vision with technical realities
  • Keep in-game assets, environments, and characters optimized for performance and quality

title: 3D Character Artist
description:
About the Role

As a character artist at Valve, you will join a world-class group of software engineers, artists, writers, animators, and designers who are continually building and releasing entertainment experiences to players around the globe. We're hoping to add your expertise in character design and sculpting to our team!

Valve is an entertainment and technology company driven to design and deliver extraordinary entertainment experiences to customers. We hire people with broad skill sets who also exhibit deep expertise. Character artists at Valve contribute in all aspects of our character pipeline: we sculpt high-resolution models (ZBrush, Mudbox or 3DCoat), craft low-resolution game models, and author high-quality textures suitable for PBR workflows. We collaborate with concept artists to iterate on character designs and work with animators to build characters that can move and perform believably. We work in interdisciplinary design teams to help bring characters to life while making them work in the context of our game design. We also recruit artists like you.

Do you prefer to collaborate with peers to define the work that you pursue, rather than simply follow a vision that's been set for you? If you're seeking an opportunity to work on genre-defining games with other world-class artists, consider joining Valve. We can't wait to see where you'll take us!

Please provide a portfolio of work which demonstrates the following:

  • High-level organic and hard-surface modeling experience in games or films
  • Your ability to model characters in a variety of styles with an emphasis on character design
  • Your direct contribution to both the conceptual thinking behind and the physical creation of detailed, high-quality products
  • A thorough understanding of human anatomy
  • Pride in and personal ownership of your work
  • Drawing, sculpting, and other traditional art skills is a plus

Sorry, we do not accept student portfolios.

Now!

What We Offer

  • An organization where 100% of time is dedicated as groups see fit
  • The opportunity to collaborate with experts across a range of disciplines
  • A work environment and flexible schedule in support of families and domestic partnerships
  • A culture eager to become stronger through diversity of all forms
  • Exceptional health insurance coverage
  • Unrivaled employer match for our 401(k) retirement plan
  • Generous vacation and family leave
  • On-site amenities in support of health and efficiency
  • Fertility and adoption assistance
  • Reimbursement for child care during interviews

title: Senior Product Designer (Typeform Ai)
description:
About the Role

We are looking for an experienced product designer to help drive the Typeform AI initiative, ensuring cohesive AI experiences, accelerating product wide adoption, and continuously improving UX and UI across capture, automation, and insights.

This role will enable the shift toward an AI native Typeform platform by designing clear, safe, and scalable experiences that help customers confidently create, manage, and extend AI powered workflows across the full product. This includes unlocking smarter authoring, automation, and insights while maintaining interoperability, governance, and trust.

You will join an already formed team focused on building and iterating on these ideas. The Typeform AI platform already exists, and this role will help build, refine, and systemize those experiences over time.

This is where you will come in.You will help shape how AI is discovered, activated, and used across the end to end Typeform journey, ensuring value is clear during creation, distribution, and insights.

Responsibilities

  • Enable the shift toward an AI native Typeform platform through clear and scalable design
  • Identify, design, and iterate on how users discover, activate, and benefit from AI across the full Typeform journey
  • Create UX and visual designs for AI driven suggestions, copilots, templates, and action flows
  • Design interaction models for inline guidance, command surfaces, and background automation
  • Collaborate closely with Product, Engineering, AI and ML, and Developer Relations to deliver consistent cross platform experiences
  • Plan and run research, workshops, and feedback sessions to validate complex workflows, including data access and permissions
  • Help evolve shared systems and patterns for AI experiences across the product
  • Use and contribute to the Echo design system to ensure accessible and production ready outcomes

Requirements

  • 5 or more years of industry experience in digital product design
  • Experience working on complex product areas with multiple stakeholders
  • Specialism in zero to one product development, not only feature improvements
  • Confidence across the full design process, from discovery through execution
  • Strong UX and visual design skills focused on clarity and usability
  • Hands on experience conducting user research, usability testing, and incorporating feedback into design iterations
  • Experience working on AI based products
  • Experience working with design systems, including contributing new patterns
  • Proven experience designing SaaS products, with a focus on marketer tools
  • A solid portfolio of case studies that demonstrate strong thinking and craft
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Fluency in English

About the Team

Typeform is a refreshingly different form builder. We help over 150,000 businesses collect the data they need with forms, surveys, and quizzes that people enjoy. Designed to look striking and feel effortless to fill out, Typeform drives 500 million responses every year—and integrates with essential tools like Slack, Zapier, and Hubspot.

We move as one team, empowering our collective efforts by valuing each individual's perspective. We champion our diverse customer base by anticipating their needs and addressing their challenges with priority. We are committed to excellence and hold high expectations for ourselves and each other, continuously striving to deliver exceptional results.

We are proud to be an equal-opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and stand against discrimination and harassment of any kind. Everyone is welcome here.

title: Senior Gameplay Designer, Clash of Clans
description:
About the Role

Clash of Clans has been live for 13 years - but we're just getting started.

Do you want to help shape the world of Clash and come up with the next set of iconic characters? We are looking for an experienced gameplaydesigner with comprehensive skills that range from developing first concepts to delivering fully-executed and polished features in a live-game environment. This is a hands-on role that requires learning, developing, and working directly on internal gameplay systems, as well as coordinating work with other developers to facilitate the highest quality and most effective content production.

Essential to this role is a deep understanding of gameplay, balancing, accessibility, and ongoing player experience within free-to-play and PvP mobile games. Your responsibilities will include driving new gameplay content and design innovations, collaborating with other designers and developers, and delivering compelling experiences to a highly-engaged player community of tens of millions of players. Design is a collective effort at Supercell, so it's paramount that you constantly seek and manage input from developers and players alike!

We offer you a chance to join a team of professionals, where talent feeds talent. We do not believe in bureaucracy or convoluted processes. We believe game development is a team sport and work together to achieve the best results. We trust each other's skills and value each other's work. All of us are driven by the amazing opportunity to make a game played by tens of millions of players better and better. If this sounds exciting to you, then you might just be the person we are looking for!

Responsibilities

  • Drive quick prototyping, playtesting, feedback iteration cycles, and explore new gameplay design space
  • Come up with compelling new gameplay mechanics in collaboration with artists, to ensure features are well-visualized, intuitive and impactful
  • Prototype and craft features in tandem with programmers, to optimize gamefeel and empower future design capability
  • Organize, backfill, and provide detailed design specs on needed work, including visual mockups, to prevent design bottlenecks in production pipelines
  • Take high-level ownership over final deliverables, to ensure the end-user experience is complete, balanced, polished, and tested
  • Iterate and improve gameplay features, and shape the way in which they'll develop in the upcoming years
  • Balance existing gameplay elements to ensure healthy engagement KPIs, and maintain a healthy state of the overall game meta
  • Contribute new design ideas on ways to engage players, and collaborate with the team on the feature roadmap
  • Share responsibility for the game's overall design vision and ensure that the whole development team shares and agrees on the vision

Requirements

  • Extensive understanding, skills and experience with gameplay design
  • Track record working with action gameplay games with moment to moment gameplay
  • Understanding of other design areas (e.g. level design) is a big plus
  • Proactive and productive way of working in collaboration with others
  • Excellent communication, interpersonal and organizational skills
  • Absolute passion for playing and delivering great gameplay experiences

Where You'll Be

This role is based either in our London Studio or Helsinki Studio. The Clash of Clans team members are located in both and we view them as dual locations.

To be successful, you'll be expected to travel quite regularly and often in this role.

About Supercell

Supercell is a games company from Helsinki, Finland, with offices also in San Francisco, Seoul, Shanghai, and London. You might know us as the makers of Hay Day, Clash of Clans, Boom Beach, Clash Royale, and Brawl Stars. Our mission is to create great games that as many people as possible play for years and that are remembered forever.

So, how do we make great games? By putting together the best teams and giving them the freedom and independence to succeed. And by taking risks, failing, sharing learnings, and killing lots of projects.

Benefits and Perks

We take good care of our people, providing them with the compensation, work environment, and resources they need to succeed while having fun along the way.

Relocation Support

No matter where you're moving from, our dedicated mobility team and partners will support you throughout your move. We'll ensure the process is as smooth as possible for you and anyone joining you - whether they're family members of the human or animal kind!

Application Encouragement

Many candidates with great skills and experience second-guess themselves. The bar is high, but if this role excites you, apply! We're here to help you succeed. We also welcome any specific accommodations you may need to fully engage in our recruitment process.

Wishing you all the best,
The Supercell Recruitment team

title: Senior Video Producer
description:
The Vox Media Podcast Network is seeking a Senior Video Producer to drive multiplatform production across a diverse and high-profile slate of podcasts. This role will serve as a key creative contributor to show teams launching new video-first shows, and support production across the existing show slate as needed.

You'll work closely with hosts, producers, and editorial stakeholders to shape each show's unique visual and conceptual identity, develop segments/formats, and lead a weekly edit workflow. This is a hands-on role for a highly skilled and versatile video editor and visual storyteller who has experience managing freelance contributors, coaching host talent, and delivering polished, high-performing content tailored to YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, and other platforms.

Responsibilities

  • Partner with podcast teams across the network to develop and launch new video shows, offering both creative consultation and execution support.
  • Edit and produce longform video episodes for platforms like YouTube, ensuring strong pacing, dynamic visuals, and high production value across remote, in-studio, and hybrid formats.
  • Create platform-native shortform video content optimized for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts-translating strong podcast moments into compelling visual stories.
  • Design and implement scalable visual elements (motion graphics, captions, lower-thirds, etc.) that align with each show's tone and target platform.
  • Manage and mentor freelance editors and designers, assigning work, giving creative feedback, and ensuring brand consistency and quality across deliverables.
  • Write and edit scripts as needed in collaboration with hosts/show teams.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with editorial, social, and audience development teams to ensure shows meet performance goals and platform best practices.
  • Stay current on visual and social trends across the podcast and digital media landscape, offering insights and ideas that help shows reach their target audiences and stand out from their competitive set.

Requirements

  • 6-8+ years of experience in digital video production, with a track record of success creating editorial or creator-driven content for social and streaming platforms.
  • Demonstrated ability to produce both longform and shortform video content, especially for talk-based or interview-driven formats.
  • Strong editing expertise in Adobe Premiere (motion design and graphics capabilities in After Effects or similar tools a plus).
  • Experience managing freelancers or vendors, and clear creative direction that ensures timely, high-quality delivery.
  • Excellent editorial instincts and scriptwriting ability, with a knack for shaping pacing, tone, and story arc, especially in host-driven formats.
  • A collaborative, consultative working style and the ability to plug into existing teams to help elevate their visual presence.
  • A deep understanding of video platform ecosystems (YouTube, TikTok, Reels), including what drives performance, how users engage, and what makes shows grow.

About the Role

The role involves leading video production efforts for podcasts, creating engaging content tailored for multiple platforms, managing freelance teams, and staying ahead of digital media trends.

title: Video Editor, A Touch More & Bird's Eye View
description:
The Vox Media Podcast Network is looking for a Video Editor to lead the visual identity and post-production for "A Touch More" and "Bird's Eye View," two flagship women's sports and lifestyle podcasts. You will own the video editing workflow for full-length episodes and collaborate on creating compelling short-form content optimized for social platforms.

The ideal candidate is a meticulous and fast technical editor who understands how podcasts move through the modern media ecosystem. You should have a strong sense of visual style and a portfolio that showcases conceptually bold work.

Responsibilities

  • Lead the end-to-end editing and visual post-production for one to two full-length podcast episodes per week, distributed on YouTube and other video platforms, and support the creation of social media clips.
  • Translate the personality and pacing of our hosts into a distinct and elevated podcast aesthetic.
  • Design and integrate dynamic motion graphics, lower thirds, captions, and visual cues that enhance the narrative and tone.
  • Ensure consistent branding through the expert use of color, typography, and layout.
  • Address mixed-location interviews (e.g., Zoom + studio footage, live event footage) with creativity, ensuring cohesion and polish regardless of source quality.
  • Implement split-screen layouts and on-screen text to keep long-form conversations visually engaging and viewer-friendly.
  • Work closely with hosts, producers, and showrunners to iterate quickly and deliver high-quality content on a strict weekly schedule.

Requirements

  • Proven experience in video podcast production for a media brand, agency, or individual creator.
  • Mastery of Adobe Premiere and a strong working knowledge of motion design, graphic design, and audio editing programs.
  • A keen eye for identifying the natural "flow" of a recording and a well-honed sense of pacing to keep viewers engaged throughout a full episode.
  • A body of work that showcases your ability to shape voice, tone, and rhythm through editing.
  • Comfort with multi-camera editing and techniques like overlays, animated inserts, and typography to emphasize clarity or humor.
  • The ability to maintain high brand standards while working against the tight deadlines of a twice-weekly show with variable recording schedules.

About the Role

We are looking for a skilled video editor with experience in podcast production, strong technical skills, and a creative eye for storytelling through visuals. The role involves managing multiple episodes per week, collaborating with a creative team, and ensuring high-quality, engaging content across platforms.

Platform Passion

Deep knowledge of the social media landscape and excitement about evolving podcasts into dynamic, multiplatform products.

title: Deputy Editor, Future Perfect
description:
About the Role

Vox Media is seeking a Deputy Editor for Future Perfect, a section dedicated to exploring how to do good in the world through evidence-driven reporting on topics like emerging technology, philanthropy, global health, animal welfare, and catastrophic risks. The role involves managing and editing three reporting fellows, providing journalism training and coaching, overseeing the newsletter, and supporting daily editorial operations. The position is full-time, 16 months, and remote (US Only).

Responsibilities

  • Manage and edit three reporting fellows, guiding story ideas, reporting plans, drafts, and professional growth.
  • Provide journalism training and coaching, including designing training sessions on various reporting skills.
  • Oversee recruitment and selection of new reporting fellows, including outreach, application review, and interview coordination.
  • Edit and shape Future Perfect's twice-weekly newsletter, ensuring quality and alignment with the section's mission.
  • Support daily editorial operations, including story calendar management, coverage gap identification, and cross-team coordination.

Requirements

  • At least 3 years of full-time editing experience, preferably in a fast-paced digital newsroom; management experience is a plus.
  • Strong ability to coach and develop early-career writers with clear feedback and trust-building skills.
  • Excellent story judgment and editing range, from story framing to polishing drafts.
  • Understanding of web publishing urgency, accuracy, and care.
  • Enthusiasm for Future Perfect's core topics and evidence-driven reporting.
  • Organized, self-starting approach capable of managing multiple tasks simultaneously.

Work Location

This is a remote position.

Salary

USD 95,000 - 105,000

title: Senior Video Producer, Unexplainable
description:
About the Role

Vox is seeking a technically skilled, creative, and organized Senior Video Producer to develop and produce a weekly video interview version of Unexplainable, Vox's award-winning narrative podcast. This is a one-year benefits eligible contract position.

About the Show

Unexplainable is a sound-rich biweekly podcast that delves into the mysteries of science with equal parts playfulness and journalistic rigor. We are looking for an experienced video producer to help us translate the show to video through engaging visuals, thoughtful editing, and journalistic best practices.

Responsibilities

  • Collaborate closely with the Unexplainable team to create a stand-out weekly video podcast
  • Develop the visual identity of the show
  • Assist in all steps of production, from brainstorming, planning, and coordinating recordings to editing and managing post-production
  • Assemble, refine, and package each episode in Premiere
  • Create content cuts for both video and audio versions
  • Work closely with producer-hosts, story editors, audio engineers, video editors, and animators

Requirements

  • Background in journalism and a curiosity about science
  • Strong editorial sensibility and technical instincts
  • Experience translating narrative audio content into engaging video

title: Graphic Designer Apprentice
description:

We are seeking a motivated and enthusiastic individual to join our team as a Level 3 Learning and Development
Practitioner Apprentice. This apprenticeship offers an excellent opportunity to develop professional

towards a nationally recognized qualification.

title: Senior/Lead UI/UX Designer - Gram Games
description:
About the Role

As a Senior/Lead UI/UX Designer at Gram Games, you will be a hands-on creative force driving the player experience for our newest mobile game projects. You will work at the heart of an experienced team, collaborating with artists, producers, and developers to define the visual language and usability of titles from their earliest stages. You are not just a strategist; you are a builder who can carry a design from a rough sketch to a polished, functional implementation in Unity.

Responsibilities

  • Produce creative, player-focused wireframes, user flows, and comprehensive supporting documentation that serve as the blueprint for new game mechanics.
  • Create stunning User Interface art assets that are flexible, efficient, and adaptable to a variety of artistic styles suitable for the "New Games" pipeline.
  • Design and directly implement user interface screens within Unity, ensuring that the final in-game experience matches the design vision.
  • Work closely with game designers and lead artists to establish and maintain a consistent, world-class visual identity for new IPs.
  • Partner with engineers and technical artists to solve complex implementation challenges and ensure designs are optimized for mobile performance.
  • Guide the work of other UI/UX artists, providing actionable feedback and fostering a culture of creative excellence.

Requirements

  • Strong hands-on experience with UI implementation in the Unity Engine (UGUI or similar) is essential.
  • Highly proficient in Photoshop and Figma for high-fidelity prototyping and asset creation.
  • Proven experience in the mobile game industry, with a deep understanding of mobile-specific design patterns and constraints.
  • Expert knowledge of UX principles with a focus on practical problem solving and rapid prototyping.
  • A portfolio demonstrating strong artistic sense, typography, color theory, and layout skills.
  • Extensive experience collaborating with engineers, artists, and product leads to balance creative vision with technical feasibility.
  • Exceptional teamwork and communication skills, with the ability to articulate design decisions to stakeholders.

Good to have

  • Knowledge of Adobe Illustrator, as well as UI animation and visual effects (VFX) skills.
  • Proactive ability to provide constructive feedback and receive it effectively to iterate on designs.
  • Experience with different methods of play-testing and the ability to translate player data into meaningful UI/UX improvements.
  • Experience working across multiple UI/UX styles simultaneously, from hyper-casual to mid-core aesthetics.

title: Senior 3D Motion Designer
description:
About the Role

Our Motion Designers bring every project to life, playing a pivotal role in delivering outstanding work that makes us proud. As a Senior 3D Motion Designer, you'll have the chance to push creative boundaries and set new standards within our agency.

We are looking for an artist who:

  • Demonstrates experience leading projects from concept through to production and delivery.
  • Has expertise in 3D and 2D projects and is proficient in 3D motion pipelines and their integration into 2D workflows.
  • Strong understanding of storytelling fundamentals and skilled in developing concepts/treatments, styleframes, storyboards and animatics.
  • Strong presentation and communication skills and confidence pitching to clients.
  • Capable of supporting on multiple projects simultaneously, whilst adhering to tight deadlines.
  • Demonstrates bold creative thinking while paying close attention to crucial details.
  • Is meticulous with a robust design background and up-to-date knowledge of current trends in motion and design.
  • Adaptable team player who can adjust their creative approach based on project scale and requirements.
  • Has a desire to push boundaries.

Key Skills and Requirements:

  • Expert level understanding of Cinema 4D, Redshift, AE, Adobe Suite
  • Unreal engine / Houdini a bonus but not required.
  • 7+ years experience delivering high-end CG content, working across the full pipeline from concept development to styleframing to compositing.
  • A generalist, with a passion for art direction, technical or both.
  • Up to date with current production workflows - organised and process-driven.
  • Strong sense of design, up to date with current design and motion trends.
  • Strong animation fundamentals, ability to work across multiple styles of animation.
  • Comfortable working at pace to deliver quickfire R&D and Lookdev.
  • Give direction and provide feedback when working with external talents.
  • Ability to lead projects coordinating and directing mid-junior designers on a day-to-day basis.
  • Serve as a mentor and role-model to junior motion designers on the team, supporting their personal development. Ensure they acquire and refine the necessary skills, tools, and processes to excel in their roles.
  • Clear communicator, has to be able to liaise with the production/leadership team, and present work for review.

How We Support You

Inertia is only ever as good as the talented people that choose to call it 'home'. We are committed to providing the support needed for people to do their best work and have fun doing it.

These are some of the things we have in place for our employees:

  • We offer 22 days paid holiday.
  • No one should have to work on their birthday, so you get that off too and if work permits, we also allow the week off between Christmas and New Year.
  • We operate a day for a day TOIL policy, should you be asked to work weekends and/or bank holidays.
  • Regular team and studio socials, including trips to key industry events and festivals.

Additional Info

  • Role type: full-time and on-site. While we do have WFH days, this role is not remote.
  • Reports to: Executive Creative Director
  • Team size: Internal team of 8 largely working out of our London office.

title: Game Design Director
description:
Position Overview

As the Design Director for Monster Legends, you will report directly to the GM and join a team that is committed to delivering great player experiences that turn into a growing business. Your mission will be to lead the design department (Systems, Level, UI/UX) and cooperate with your close product/engineering / art/production peers to define long term game goals, draft a strategy and follow up the execution.

  • Proven track record of managing a design or product team that delivered business results in a sustained way

title: Game Animator
description:
Position Overview:

You will be working as part of a dedicated in-house team responsible for building amazing game animations that wow our players. Working in a highly motivated team you will have an opportunity to develop and share your skills, learn from experienced peers and collaborate with designers and artists to craft amazing in-game animations.

Responsibilities

What We Offer You

  • Extremely talented and helpful colleagues
  • Lots of power and responsibility

title: Video Producer, Vulture
description:
Vulture is expanding its video operation with the hiring of a Video Producer. This is a high-impact, hands-on role for an ambitious producer/editor who can create standout video storytelling, establish production systems, and translate Vulture's editorial voice into new formats and platforms. You'll work from our New York office and collaborate closely with editorial, social, audience, and Vox Studios partners, helping to guide the strategy, standards, and day-to-day workflows for video at Vulture.

WHO WE ARE

Vulture, New York Magazine's home for pop culture and entertainment coverage, is where savvy culture consumers come to find sharp, obsessive coverage of their favorite entertainment and the people who create it.

Vulture is part of Vox Media, the leading modern media company. We guide our audience from discovery to obsession. We inspire essential conversations about what's now, what's next, and what's possible.

As a community of journalists and storytellers, business professionals, creators and technologists, we believe it is a moral and business imperative to amplify voices: to cultivate diversity, equity, and inclusion throughout our organization and media. This applies to our candidates, our teams, our storytelling, our creative work, and our platforms, products, and partnerships.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

  • Own end-to-end video production for Vulture: conceive, pitch, produce, shoot, edit and deliver ambitious short- and long-form video across web, YouTube, and social platforms.
  • Help add structure to Vulture's video program: create production templates, naming conventions, file-delivery standards, budgeting processes, and a rollout calendar.
  • Produce studio and remote shoots: coordinate with Vox Studios to secure gear, set up and operate camera, lighting and sound, or hire and oversee shooters and crew for field shoots.
  • Edit videos for horizontal and vertical formats, including color correction, audio mix, and simple motion graphics.
  • Design or commission thumbnails and title sequences.
  • Build a roster of freelance videographers, editors, and motion designers; negotiate rates and scope, and commission external teams as needed.
  • Source and book talent, manage on-set logistics, and liaise with publicists and agents.
  • Collaborate across teams: work with editors, social editors, the audience team, and product to optimize storytelling, distribution, and engagement strategy.
  • Use metrics and feedback to iterate on formats, timing, and promotion; run post-mortems and use performance data to inform future ideas.
  • Ensure legal and clearance processes are followed for music, clips and talent releases, working with Legal/Clearance as needed.
  • Troubleshoot technical and production challenges in real time and maintain production equipment or vendor relationships.

WHO YOU ARE

  • Proven track record producing and editing digital video for editorial or branded platforms.
  • Strong hands-on experience with camera, lighting and sound setup and operation; comfortable running shoots alone or leading small crews.
  • Proficiency in Adobe Premiere Pro and After Effects; strong sense for pacing, story, and visual design.
  • Demonstrated ability to shoot and edit for both horizontal (YouTube/web) and vertical (TikTok/Reels) formats.
  • Experience creating motion graphics, title sequences and thumbnails.
  • Excellent project management skills - able to juggle multiple projects, timelines, freelancers and stakeholders simultaneously.
  • Comfortable working autonomously and making editorial and production decisions as the primary video lead.
  • Strong communication skills and the ability to partner with editors, social, audience, legal and external vendors.
  • A portfolio or reel demonstrating producing and editing credits and your role on each project.

Preferred

  • 4-6+ years producing and editing digital video; experience launching or leading a small video initiative or team.
  • Experience building vendor networks and managing freelance rosters.
  • Familiarity with codecs, transcodes, deliverables, metadata, and media asset management best practices.
  • Basic audio mixing, color grading, and motion design skills beyond cut-level editing.
  • Experience using analytics tools to measure video performance and inform editorial decisions.
  • Prior experience booking high-profile talent or handling talent logistics for editorial shoots.

Why this role matters

This role is ideal for a self-starter who is excited by both the craft of making distinctive video and the work of building processes, systems, and relationships that let that work scale. You'll have editorial freedom to experiment, influence Vulture's multimedia strategy, and shape how our culture coverage lives on screen.

WHERE YOU'LL WORK

This job is based in our New York, NY office.

title: Brand Campaign Manager
description:
Level Up Your Career with Zynga!

At Zynga, we bring people together through the power of play. As a global leader in interactive entertainment and a proud label of Take-Two Interactive, our games have been downloaded over 6 billion times-connecting players in 175+ countries through fun, strategy, and a little friendly competition.

From thrilling casino spins to epic strategy battles, mind-bending puzzles, and social word challenges, our diverse game portfolio has something for everyone. Fan-favorites and latest hits include FarmVille™, Words With Friends™, Zynga Poker™, Game of Thrones Slots Casino™, Wizard of Oz Slots™, Hit it Rich! Slots™, Wonka Slots™, Top Eleven™, Toon Blast™, Empires & Puzzles™, Merge Dragons!™, CSR Racing™, Harry Potter: Puzzles & Spells™, Match Factory™, and Color Block Jam™-plus many more!

Founded in 2007 and headquartered in California, our teams span North America, Europe, and Asia, working together to craft unforgettable gaming experiences. Whether you're spinning, strategizing, matching, or competing, Zynga is where fun meets innovation-and where you can take your career to the next level.

Join us and be part of the play!

Position Overview:

We are seeking a Manager, Brand Campaign to join our expanding Brand Central business unit. We are looking for a tenacious and proactive manager focused on delivering Brand campaigns across Paid Brand Media across an ever evolving gaming portfolio whilst supporting co-promotional partnerships - driving both brand equity and player growth through integrated, creative, and measurable campaigns. The ideal candidate is a proactive, hands-on marketer with strong media expertise, capable of driving brand equity and player growth through integrated, creative, and measurable campaigns in a fast-paced environment.

We embrace a hybrid work environment. Within this role you will work 2 days per week in our London office and the remaining days at home.

What You'll Do:

Brand Media Strategy & Vision (Primary Focus)

  • Partner with Brand Strategy Lead to define upper- and mid-funnel brand media strategy across portfolio titles.
  • Collaborate with UA leadership to ensure brand media efforts complement and support lower-funnel performance initiatives.
  • Support Consumer Insights initiatives, including testing frameworks, brief development, and content delivery.
  • Identify opportunities to evolve brand storytelling and media approaches across platforms and markets.

Brand Media Campaign Execution & Cross-Functional Collaboration

  • Own end-to-end execution of paid Brand Media campaigns, including briefing, timelines, approvals, launch readiness, and post-campaign reporting.
  • Lead day-to-day brand media planning with a hybrid model, working across media agencies and direct platform partners (Google, Meta, TikTok, etc.).
  • Collaborate closely with Brand Studio, UA, Influencer, Creative, Product Marketing, and Live Ops teams to ensure cohesive execution.
  • Serve as day-to-day point of contact with Business Operations for POs, NDAs, and key legal workflows (e.g., new agency onboarding).
  • Ensure all media activity aligns with brand positioning, creative standards, and player experience expectations.

Brand Promotional Activations Support (Secondary Focus)

  • Support in executing large-scale brand co-promotions, including in-game, social, paid, and owned-channel activations.
  • Assist with co-promotional partnerships, ensuring strong integration with broader brand media efforts.
  • Contribute to ideation and execution of new activation or integration opportunities across the portfolio.

Agency & Partner Management

  • Act as lead point of contact for brand media agencies, briefing and refining proposals in alignment with leadership priorities.
  • Build and maintain strong relationships with agencies and platform partners.
  • Maintain and update Portfolio Games' Brand Partnership Guidelines and ensure brand safety compliance across campaigns.

Measurement, Reporting & Impact

  • Define clear KPIs aligned to campaign objectives; monitor performance and optimize against results.
  • Partner with Marketing Analytics to ensure tracking readiness (e.g., brand lift studies, in-game surveys, tracking tools).
  • Deliver post-campaign reporting with actionable insights and recommendations to inform future brand investments.

Knowledge Sharing & Team Contribution

  • Support Leads with internal quarterly updates across the Brand Central division.
  • Contribute to the development of a monthly industry news and trends newsletter.
  • Proactively surface relevant industry changes, platform updates, and emerging marketing trends.

What You Bring:

  • 5+ years of experience in general Brand Management or Integrated Marketing
  • Proven experience leading high-impact brand media campaigns, ideally within gaming, entertainment, or consumer tech
  • Strong understanding of upper- and mid-funnel marketing and how it supports performance outcomes
  • Experience collaborating with agencies, platform partners, and cross-functional teams
  • Familiarity with co-marketing partnerships and influencer marketing, or a strong willingness to expand in these areas
  • Understanding of mobile gaming audiences and free-to-play business models
  • Entrepreneurial mindset with comfort operating in ambiguity
  • Excellent communication, presentation (slide decks), and project management skills

Bonus Points:

  • Experience in mobile gaming, apps, or entertainment
  • Experience supporting influencer-led or creator-based partnerships
  • Exposure to global campaigns across multiple regions

What We Offer You:

  • Competitive salary and discretionary annual bonus scheme
  • 26 days holiday, plus bank holidays & 2 paid personal days
  • 6 months fully paid maternity/adoption leave and 16 weeks fully paid paternity leave (eligibility criteria applies)
  • Pension plan, with a double matching employer contribution
  • Private medical & dental insurance and healthcare cash plan
  • Life insurance, personal accident insurance and income protection
  • Mental health and neurodiversity support programs
  • Company Employee Stock Purchase Plan (eligibility criteria applies)
  • Fitness reimbursement plan, worth £600 per annum
  • Flexible working hours
  • Weekly happy hours, seasonal parties and monthly social events
  • Annual rail season ticket advance and cycle to work scheme
